You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). You have Bash, Read, Grep tools.

STEP 1 — get your file list. Run:
  /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[5]; print(chr(10).join(s))"
That prints your assigned slice as lines of "relpath:START-END" (relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent).

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every one of your assigned files/ranges, line by line, using the Read tool with the given offset/limit. Do NOT grep-and-skim. Actually read the code so you catch names a keyword search would miss.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (module/file name, class, function/method, variable, constant, enum value) that would look UNPROFESSIONAL in an external code review. This includes:
  - marketing / quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, proper(when boastful), aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best, clean(when boastful)
  - temporal / version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(when it means 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes / informal / vague: hack, yolo, stuff, misc, foo, doit, thing, blah, magic numbers named as words
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME, not KEEP.** Only mark KEEP for names that are unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a real domain term (e.g. 'finalize', 'proper_noun', 'evict_older_than', a real product name like 'SmartArt' ONLY if the code truly implements it).

STEP 4 — WRITE your findings by APPENDING TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_5.tsv, one row per flagged name:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY_it_reads_unprofessional<TAB>SUGGESTED_professional_name
  (VERDICT = RENAME or KEEP-BUT-NOTED; KIND = file/class/func/var/const/enum)
Use a bash heredoc or printf with real tab characters. If a file range has NO issues, still write one row: NONE<TAB>-<TAB><relpath><TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-.

Return ONLY a one-line summary: "shard 5: <R> renames, <K> kept-noted across <F> files".

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	"""I-ret-002 (#1294) layer 2 (fetch_crawl) — bake-off runner + scorer.
2	
3	CANDIDATE ENGINES (exact pip / API ids, web-verified 2026-06-23):
4	  * crawl4ai (BASELINE)  — pip ``crawl4ai`` (>=0.6); Playwright-Chromium markdown extractor.
5	                           Wired in prod via ``AccessBypass._try_crawl4ai``.
6	  * zyte                 — pip ``zyte-api`` (``from zyte_api import ZyteAPI``); browserHtml
7	                           anti-bot solver. Needs ``ZYTE_API_KEY`` (no_key => registered-but-skipped,
8	                           NEVER faked). Wired in prod via ``AccessBypass._try_zyte``.
9	  * firecrawl            — pip ``firecrawl-py`` (``from firecrawl import FirecrawlApp``). Needs
10	                           ``FIRECRAWL_API_KEY`` (no_key => skipped). Hosted scrape API.
11	  * playwright           — pip ``playwright`` (``playwright install chromium``). Headless browser.
12	                           ``needs_gpu`` is FALSE for fetch, but it ``needs_browser`` (a Chromium
13	                           runtime) — gated behind a runtime check, honestly skipped if absent.
14	
15	METRIC (per brief, §-1.1-compliant — REAL engine output vs LABELED ground truth):
16	  Per-URL recovery verdict {RECOVERED / WALLED / SOFT_STUB / FETCH_FAIL} scored vs the gold
17	  ``recovery_class`` (recovery-verdict accuracy + a RECOVERED-recall view), PLUS main-content
18	  reference-recall: the Jaccard / recall of the engine body's content-token SET against the gold
19	  ``reference_tokens`` SET. NEVER a length floor — a long junk page recalls ~0 of the gold tokens;
20	  a short faithful extract recalls high. The composite score is recovery-correctness gated by
21	  reference-recall on the RECOVERED rows (a believable verdict that recovers none of the gold body
22	  is not a real recovery).
23	
24	HONEST SCOPE (LAW II): a missing-key engine is ``no_key`` (registered, listed, SKIPPED — its score
25	is ``null`` with ``status=no_key``, never a believable-low number). A missing browser runtime is
26	``needs_browser`` (skipped). The GATE-0 per-engine liveness canary (gate0.py) is what FAILS LOUD
27	on a keyless/stub engine; this runner records the honest status so a skipped engine is never ranked
28	above a real one.
29	
30	WEIGHT-NOT-FILTER: this layer changes NO production drop. ``tier`` rides along as a surfaced weight.
31	Faithfulness engine untouched.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import argparse
37	import json
38	import os
39	import time
40	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
41	from typing import Any, Callable
42	
43	from _polaris_root import ensure_on_syspath
44	
45	_POLARIS_ROOT = ensure_on_syspath()
46	
47	# Reuse the fixture loader + the recovery rubric + the content-token tokenizer (single source).
48	from build_fixture import (  # noqa: E402
49	    FETCH_FAIL,
50	    RECOVERED,
51	    RECOVERY_CLASSES,
52	    SOFT_STUB,
53	    WALLED,
54	    classify_recovery,
55	    content_tokens,
56	    load_fixture,
57	)
58	
59	# ── Status vocabulary for an engine's run on a URL / overall ────────────────────────────
60	STATUS_SCORED = "scored"
61	STATUS_NO_KEY = "no_key"            # registered but missing API key — SKIPPED, never faked.
62	STATUS_NEEDS_BROWSER = "needs_browser"  # missing Chromium/Playwright runtime — SKIPPED.
63	STATUS_LOAD_FAIL = "load_fail"      # the engine package failed to import — SKIPPED, surfaced loud.
64	
65	# Default fixture + results paths (this layer's directory only).
66	_HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
67	_DEFAULT_FIXTURE = os.path.join(_HERE, "fetch_crawl_refbody_fixture.jsonl")
68	_DEFAULT_RESULTS = os.path.join(_HERE, "fetch_crawl_bakeoff_results.json")
69	
70	# Reference-recall floor below which a "RECOVERED" verdict is NOT counted as a true recovery (the
71	# body recovered too little of the gold main content to be the article). A *recall* threshold on a
72	# TOKEN-SET overlap, NOT a length floor: a faithful short extract clears it; a long junk page fails
73	# it. LAW VI env-overridable. This gates the recovery verdict; it never drops a candidate.
74	_ENV_REFRECALL_MIN = "PG_FETCH_REFRECALL_MIN"
75	_DEFAULT_REFRECALL_MIN = 0.30
76	
77	
78	def _refrecall_min() -> float:
79	    try:
80	        v = float(os.environ.get(_ENV_REFRECALL_MIN, _DEFAULT_REFRECALL_MIN) or _DEFAULT_REFRECALL_MIN)
81	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
82	        return _DEFAULT_REFRECALL_MIN
83	    return v if 0.0 <= v <= 1.0 else _DEFAULT_REFRECALL_MIN
84	
85	
86	# ── The scorer math (also imported by gate0.py + smoke_test.py) ─────────────────────────
87	
88	def reference_recall(engine_body: str, gold_tokens: set[str]) -> float:
89	    """Recall of the gold reference-token SET by the engine body's content-token SET.
90	
91	    = |gold ∩ engine| / |gold|. A pure SET overlap (the §-1.1 "real output vs labeled gold"),
92	    NOT a length comparison. Empty gold => 0.0 (cannot measure recall of nothing).
93	    """
94	    if not gold_tokens:
95	        return 0.0
96	    eng = set(content_tokens(engine_body or ""))
97	    if not eng:
98	        return 0.0
99	    hit = len(gold_tokens & eng)
100	    return hit / len(gold_tokens)
101	
102	
103	def score_url(
104	    engine_body: str,
105	    gold_recovery_class: str,
106	    gold_tokens: set[str],
107	    reference_trustworthy: bool | None = None,
108	) -> dict[str, Any]:
109	    """Score one engine fetch of one URL — RECOVER-AS-MUCH-AS-POSSIBLE framing.
110	
111	    THE OBJECTIVE IS RECOVERY, NOT REPRODUCING THE INCUMBENT. The gold body is the incumbent
112	    (crawl4ai-first AccessBypass) fetch outcome, so a gold-WALLED / gold-SOFT_STUB / gold-FETCH_FAIL
113	    row is one the INCUMBENT FAILED on — there is NO trustworthy reference body for it. An engine
114	    that turns such a row into RECOVERED real content is a WIN (the crown-jewel discriminator: Zyte
115	    beating a paywall), NEVER scored wrong. Scoring "engine == incumbent gold" would reward
116	    reproducing the incumbent and PUNISH beating it — a §-1.3 inversion (penalizing a valid better
117	    source). So:
118	
119	      * ``recovered``  : the engine returned RECOVERED real content (the per-URL recovery WIN).
120	      * ``wall_broken``: the engine RECOVERED a row the incumbent did NOT (gold != RECOVERED) — the
121	                         discriminator that the whole bake-off (and Zyte as a candidate) exists for.
122	      * ``reference_recall`` : main-content fidelity vs the gold body — MEANINGFUL ONLY where the
123	                         reference is trustworthy (gold == RECOVERED, the incumbent got full text).
124	                         On a non-trustworthy row the gold body is a shell, so recall vs it is
125	                         intentionally NOT used to judge the engine (set ``reference_meaningful``).
126	
127	    ``reference_trustworthy`` defaults to (gold_recovery_class == RECOVERED) when not given.
128	    A predicted RECOVERED that, on a TRUSTWORTHY row, recovers too little of the trustworthy
129	    reference is downgraded to SOFT_STUB (it returned a long wrong-page, not the article). On a
130	    non-trustworthy row there is no trustworthy reference to fall below, so no downgrade applies.
131	    """
132	    if reference_trustworthy is None:
133	        reference_trustworthy = gold_recovery_class == RECOVERED
134	    predicted = classify_recovery(engine_body)
135	    recall = reference_recall(engine_body, gold_tokens)
136	    effective = predicted
137	    # Only a TRUSTWORTHY reference can fail a RECOVERED prediction down to SOFT_STUB (wrong-page
138	    # guard). Against a shell gold body, low recall is EXPECTED for a real article and must NOT
139	    # downgrade the engine (that was the inversion).
140	    if predicted == RECOVERED and reference_trustworthy and recall < _refrecall_min():
141	        effective = SOFT_STUB
142	    recovered = effective == RECOVERED
143	    wall_broken = recovered and (gold_recovery_class != RECOVERED)
144	    return {
145	        "predicted_recovery_class": predicted,
146	        "effective_recovery_class": effective,
147	        "reference_recall": round(recall, 4),
148	        "reference_meaningful": bool(reference_trustworthy),
149	        "recovered": bool(recovered),
150	        "wall_broken": bool(wall_broken),
151	        "gold_recovery_class": gold_recovery_class,
152	    }
153	
154	
155	def aggregate(per_url: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
156	    """Aggregate per-URL scores into an engine's layer metric (recover-as-much-as-possible).
157	
158	    Headline metrics (none rewards reproducing the incumbent; all reward MORE recovery):
159	      * ``recovery_rate``           — fraction of ALL URLs the engine returns RECOVERED content for.
160	                                      This is the bake-off objective: recover as much as possible.
161	      * ``wall_break_rate``         — of the incumbent-FAILED rows (gold != RECOVERED), the fraction
162	                                      the engine turns into RECOVERED. The crown-jewel discriminator.
163	      * ``recovered_refrecall_macro`` — main-content fidelity on the rows with a TRUSTWORTHY
164	                                      reference (gold == RECOVERED): did the engine recover the
165	                                      actual article body, not a wrong long page.
166	
167	    ``score`` = recovery_rate blended with fidelity-where-measurable, so an engine that recovers
168	    many bodies AND keeps high main-content fidelity wins; an engine that returns long wrong pages
169	    (high recovery_rate, low fidelity) is penalized, and an engine that reproduces incumbent walls
170	    (low recovery_rate) loses. Beating a wall can only ever HELP a score, never hurt it.
171	    """
172	    if not per_url:
173	        return {
174	            "n": 0,
175	            "recovery_rate": 0.0,
176	            "wall_break_rate": 0.0,
177	            "n_incumbent_failed": 0,
178	            "recovered_refrecall_macro": 0.0,
179	            "score": 0.0,
180	            "verdict_distribution": {c: 0 for c in RECOVERY_CLASSES},
181	        }
182	    n = len(per_url)
183	    recovery_rate = sum(1 for r in per_url if r["recovered"]) / n
184	
185	    incumbent_failed = [r for r in per_url if r["gold_recovery_class"] != RECOVERED]
186	    if incumbent_failed:
187	        wall_break_rate = sum(1 for r in incumbent_failed if r["wall_broken"]) / len(incumbent_failed)
188	    else:
189	        wall_break_rate = 0.0
190	
191	    # Fidelity only where a trustworthy reference exists (gold RECOVERED rows).
192	    trustworthy = [r for r in per_url if r["reference_meaningful"]]
193	    if trustworthy:
194	        recovered_refrecall = sum(r["reference_recall"] for r in trustworthy) / len(trustworthy)
195	    else:
196	        recovered_refrecall = 0.0
197	
198	    # Verdict distribution of the ENGINE's effective classes (audit surface, NOT a quality-by-count
199	    # proxy: it is the engine's own output-class breakdown, used to read the recovery profile).
200	    verdict_distribution: dict[str, int] = {c: 0 for c in RECOVERY_CLASSES}
201	    for r in per_url:
202	        e = r["effective_recovery_class"]
203	        if e in verdict_distribution:
204	            verdict_distribution[e] += 1
205	
206	    # Composite: recovery_rate is the objective; fidelity gates it so a wrong-page flood cannot win.
207	    score = round(0.6 * recovery_rate + 0.4 * recovered_refrecall, 4)
208	    return {
209	        "n": n,
210	        "recovery_rate": round(recovery_rate, 4),
211	        "wall_break_rate": round(wall_break_rate, 4),
212	        "n_incumbent_failed": len(incumbent_failed),
213	        "recovered_refrecall_macro": round(recovered_refrecall, 4),
214	        "score": score,
215	        "verdict_distribution": verdict_distribution,
216	    }
217	
218	
219	# ── Engine registry ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
220	
221	@dataclass
222	class EngineSpec:
223	    """An engine candidate: how to identify it, gate it, and (if live) fetch with it."""
224	
225	    name: str
226	    pip_id: str
227	    import_name: str
228	    is_baseline: bool = False
229	    needs_key_env: str | None = None        # e.g. ZYTE_API_KEY / FIRECRAWL_API_KEY
230	    needs_browser: bool = False             # requires a Chromium runtime
231	    # fetch(url) -> body str; set at availability time. Returns "" on a genuine FETCH_FAIL.
232	    fetch: Callable[[str], str] | None = field(default=None, repr=False)
233	
234	
235	# Exact, web-verified ids. crawl4ai is the BASELINE (prod default free cascade).
236	def engine_specs() -> list[EngineSpec]:
237	    return [
238	        EngineSpec(name="crawl4ai", pip_id="crawl4ai>=0.6.0", import_name="crawl4ai",
239	                   is_baseline=True, needs_browser=True),
240	        EngineSpec(name="zyte", pip_id="zyte-api>=0.7.0", import_name="zyte_api",
241	                   needs_key_env="ZYTE_API_KEY"),
242	        EngineSpec(name="firecrawl", pip_id="firecrawl-py>=2.0.0", import_name="firecrawl",
243	                   needs_key_env="FIRECRAWL_API_KEY"),
244	        EngineSpec(name="playwright", pip_id="playwright>=1.45.0", import_name="playwright",
245	                   needs_browser=True),
246	    ]
247	
248	
249	def _module_importable(import_name: str) -> bool:
250	    import importlib.util
251	
252	    try:
253	        return importlib.util.find_spec(import_name) is not None
254	    except (ImportError, ValueError):
255	        return False
256	
257	
258	def engine_availability(spec: EngineSpec) -> str:
259	    """Honest availability status for an engine WITHOUT running it (LAW II).
260	
261	    Order: missing key (no_key) > package not importable (load_fail) > browser engine with no
262	    browser runtime (needs_browser) > scored (runnable). A no_key / load_fail / needs_browser engine
263	    is SKIPPED and recorded as such — never faked, never a believable-low score.
264	    """
265	    if spec.needs_key_env and not os.environ.get(spec.needs_key_env):
266	        return STATUS_NO_KEY
267	    if not _module_importable(spec.import_name):
268	        return STATUS_LOAD_FAIL
269	    if spec.needs_browser and not _chromium_runtime_present():
270	        return STATUS_NEEDS_BROWSER
271	    return STATUS_SCORED
272	
273	
274	def _chromium_runtime_present() -> bool:
275	    """True iff a Playwright/crawl4ai Chromium runtime is installed (best-effort, no launch)."""
276	    try:
277	        import importlib.util
278	
279	        if importlib.util.find_spec("playwright") is None:
280	            return False
281	        # The browser binaries live under ms-playwright; existence of the install dir is the cheap
282	        # runtime check (we do NOT launch a browser here — that is the live run's job, not the gate's).
283	        from playwright._impl._driver import compute_driver_executable  # type: ignore
284	
285	        driver = compute_driver_executable()
286	        return bool(driver)
287	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — any failure means "no usable browser runtime".
288	        return False
289	
290	
291	# ── Live fetch adapters (only invoked for a SCORED engine on the real VM run) ────────────
292	# These are intentionally thin and import lazily so the runner imports cleanly offline. They are
293	# NOT exercised by the offline smoke (which mocks them). They reuse the production fetch seams.
294	
295	def _fetch_via_polaris_zyte(url: str) -> str:
296	    """Zyte browser fetch via the production AccessBypass seam (reuse, no fork)."""
297	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import _force_zyte_refetch
298	
299	    return _force_zyte_refetch(url)
300	
301	
302	def _fetch_via_crawl4ai(url: str) -> str:
303	    """crawl4ai fetch via the production AccessBypass seam."""
304	    from src.tools.access_bypass import AccessBypass, polaris_asyncio_run
305	
306	    bypass = AccessBypass()
307	
308	    async def _run() -> Any:
309	        return await bypass._try_crawl4ai(url)
310	
311	    res = polaris_asyncio_run(_run())
312	    return str(getattr(res, "content", "") or "") if getattr(res, "success", False) else ""
313	
314	
315	def _fetch_via_firecrawl(url: str) -> str:
316	    """Firecrawl hosted scrape (markdown). Lazy import; key already asserted by availability."""
317	    from firecrawl import FirecrawlApp  # type: ignore
318	
319	    app = FirecrawlApp(api_key=os.environ["FIRECRAWL_API_KEY"])
320	    doc = app.scrape_url(url, params={"formats": ["markdown"]})
321	    if isinstance(doc, dict):
322	        return str(doc.get("markdown") or doc.get("content") or "")
323	    return str(getattr(doc, "markdown", "") or "")
324	
325	
326	def _fetch_via_playwright(url: str) -> str:
327	    """Raw Playwright headless fetch (page text). Lazy import; browser asserted by availability."""
328	    from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright  # type: ignore
329	
330	    with sync_playwright() as p:
331	        browser = p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
332	        try:
333	            page = browser.new_page()
334	            page.goto(url, wait_until="domcontentloaded", timeout=45000)
335	            return page.inner_text("body")
336	        finally:
337	            browser.close()
338	
339	
340	_FETCH_ADAPTERS: dict[str, Callable[[str], str]] = {
341	    "crawl4ai": _fetch_via_crawl4ai,
342	    "zyte": _fetch_via_polaris_zyte,
343	    "firecrawl": _fetch_via_firecrawl,
344	    "playwright": _fetch_via_playwright,
345	}
346	
347	
348	# ── Run loop ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
349	
350	def run_engine(
351	    spec: EngineSpec,
352	    fixture: list[dict[str, Any]],
353	    fetch_override: Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
354	) -> dict[str, Any]:
355	    """Run one engine across the fixture. Honest: a non-scored engine is recorded SKIPPED.
356	
357	    ``fetch_override`` lets the smoke test inject a mocked fetch (so no network). For the real run,
358	    the adapter from ``_FETCH_ADAPTERS`` is used.
359	    """
360	    status = STATUS_SCORED if fetch_override is not None else engine_availability(spec)
361	    if status != STATUS_SCORED:
362	        return {
363	            "engine": spec.name,
364	            "pip_id": spec.pip_id,
365	            "is_baseline": spec.is_baseline,
366	            "status": status,
367	            "score": None,            # never a believable-low number for a skipped engine
368	            "metric": None,
369	            "note": _skip_note(spec, status),
370	        }
371	
372	    fetch = fetch_override or _FETCH_ADAPTERS[spec.name]
373	    per_url: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
374	    for row in fixture:
375	        url = row["url"]
376	        gold_class = row["recovery_class"]
377	        gold_tokens = set(row.get("reference_tokens") or [])
378	        t0 = time.time()
379	        try:
380	            body = fetch(url)
381	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a fetch error is a FETCH_FAIL for THIS url, never aborts.
382	            body = ""
383	            err = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
384	        else:
385	            err = ""
386	        # A trustworthy reference body exists only where the INCUMBENT got full text (gold RECOVERED).
387	        reference_trustworthy = gold_class == RECOVERED
388	        scored = score_url(body, gold_class, gold_tokens, reference_trustworthy=reference_trustworthy)
389	        scored.update({"url": url, "source_type": row.get("source_type"),
390	                       "tier": row.get("tier"), "elapsed_s": round(time.time() - t0, 3),
391	                       "fetch_error": err})
392	        per_url.append(scored)
393	
394	    metric = aggregate(per_url)
395	    return {
396	        "engine": spec.name,
397	        "pip_id": spec.pip_id,
398	        "is_baseline": spec.is_baseline,
399	        "status": STATUS_SCORED,
400	        "score": metric["score"],
401	        "metric": metric,
402	        "per_url": per_url,
403	    }
404	
405	
406	def _skip_note(spec: EngineSpec, status: str) -> str:
407	    if status == STATUS_NO_KEY:
408	        return (f"SKIPPED: {spec.needs_key_env} is unset — engine registered but not run (no faked "
409	                f"score). Set the key on the VM to score it.")
410	    if status == STATUS_LOAD_FAIL:
411	        return (f"SKIPPED: package {spec.pip_id!r} (import {spec.import_name!r}) is not importable — "
412	                f"install it on the VM to score it.")
413	    if status == STATUS_NEEDS_BROWSER:
414	        return (f"SKIPPED: a Chromium runtime is absent — run `playwright install chromium` on the "
415	                f"VM to score this browser engine.")
416	    return "SKIPPED."
417	
418	
419	def rank_results(results: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
420	    """Rank SCORED engines by score desc; skipped engines listed after (score None never ranks high)."""
421	    scored = [r for r in results if r["status"] == STATUS_SCORED and r["score"] is not None]
422	    skipped = [r for r in results if not (r["status"] == STATUS_SCORED and r["score"] is not None)]
423	    scored.sort(key=lambda r: r["score"], reverse=True)
424	    return scored + skipped
425	
426	
427	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
428	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run the fetch_crawl bake-off and write ranked results.")
429	    parser.add_argument("--fixture", default=_DEFAULT_FIXTURE, help="labeled fixture JSONL")
430	    parser.add_argument("--out", default=_DEFAULT_RESULTS, help="ranked results JSON output")
431	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
432	
433	    fixture = load_fixture(args.fixture)
434	    results = [run_engine(spec, fixture) for spec in engine_specs()]
435	    ranked = rank_results(results)
436	
437	    payload = {
438	        "layer": "fetch_crawl",
439	        "issue": "I-ret-002 (#1294)",
440	        "fixture": args.fixture,
441	        "fixture_n": len(fixture),
442	        "refrecall_min": _refrecall_min(),
443	        "ranked": [
444	            {k: v for k, v in r.items() if k != "per_url"} for r in ranked
445	        ],
446	        "detail": {r["engine"]: r.get("per_url") for r in ranked if r.get("per_url")},
447	    }
448	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(args.out), exist_ok=True)
449	    with open(args.out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
450	        json.dump(payload, handle, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
451	
452	    print(f"[run_bakeoff] fixture_n={len(fixture)} -> {args.out}")
453	    for r in ranked:
454	        print(f"  {r['engine']:<12} status={r['status']:<14} score={r['score']}")
455	    return 0
456	
457	
458	if __name__ == "__main__":
459	    raise SystemExit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Build the LABELED ground-truth fixture for the quality_weight bake-off (I-ret-002 #1294, §4).
3	
4	Output: clinical_quality_weight_fixture.jsonl — one row per source drawn from the banked
5	corpus_snapshot.json bodies, each carrying the post-extraction body text + an OBJECTIVE,
6	pre-registered quality label.
7	
8	WHAT THE FIXTURE TESTS (brief §4): does a learned content-quality classifier RANK authoritative
9	clinical sources above on-topic-SEO/shallow sources, WITHIN the same topic AND within the same
10	source-type? AUC is computed over (topic_id x source_type) cells (gate0.paired_within_cell_auc),
11	so the score measures QUALITY separation, never a topic or source-type prior (§-1.1 proxy).
12	
13	LABEL INTEGRITY (the Codex iter-1 P1 — do NOT soften):
14	  * The SCORED label is set by a PRE-REGISTERED OBJECTIVE rubric on VERIFIABLE signals
15	    (peer-reviewed venue / gov-agency / guideline-body domain identity vs known SEO-spam /
16	    social / content-farm domain) — NOT "reads high-quality", NOT POLARIS tier metadata.
17	  * This deterministic rubric is FAMILY-A and it PROPOSES rows only. Per row we record:
18	        rubric_label        — the objective-rubric proposal (family A, deterministic here)
19	        family_a_label      — = rubric_label (the Claude-family proposal)
20	        family_b_label      — None until Codex family adjudication is wired (out-of-band)
21	        operator_spotcheck  — None until the operator stratified spot-check is done
22	        label_status        — "proposed" until two-family + spot-check => "adjudicated"
23	        scored_label        — None while proposed; set ONLY when label_status == "adjudicated"
24	  * run_bakeoff.py scores ONLY rows with label_status == "adjudicated" (it fails loud / flags
25	    provisional otherwise) — a rubric proposal is NEVER silently used as the scored label.
26	  * The labels are INDEPENDENT of all candidate classifiers AND of tier_classifier /
27	    authority_model (no circularity, the drb_72 fake-real-number trap).
28	
29	This script is fully OFFLINE and deterministic: the family-A objective-rubric PROPOSAL is doable
30	now from the banked snapshots; the family-B (Codex) adjudication + operator spot-check are
31	out-of-band and reported as an honest blocker (label_status stays "proposed").
32	
33	LABEL_SETS reuse (the named seam): scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py LABEL_SETS is reused ONLY
34	to confirm a body is ON-TOPIC for its question (both classes must be on-topic — a "good clinical
35	vs random junk" fixture would measure topic relevance, the reranker layer's job). It is NEVER
36	used to set the QUALITY label.
37	"""
38	
39	from __future__ import annotations
40	
41	import argparse
42	import hashlib
43	import json
44	import os
45	import sys
46	from urllib.parse import urlparse
47	
48	_HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
49	# repo root = .../scripts/dr_benchmark/retrieval_bakeoff/quality_weight -> up 4
50	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(_HERE, "..", "..", "..", ".."))
51	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:
52	    sys.path.insert(0, _REPO_ROOT)
53	
54	# Reuse the named LABEL_SETS seam for ON-TOPIC confirmation only (never for the quality label).
55	try:
56	    from scripts.relevance_scorer_bakeoff import LABEL_SETS, label_rows
57	except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - import-time guard so smoke can stub it
58	    LABEL_SETS = {}
59	    label_rows = None  # type: ignore
60	
61	DEFAULT_SNAPSHOT_ROOT = os.path.join("outputs", "corpus_backups", "extracted")
62	DEFAULT_SLUGS = [
63	    "drb_75_metal_ions_cvd",
64	    "drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc",
65	    "drb_78_parkinsons_dbs",
66	    "drb_72_ai_labor",
67	]
68	DEFAULT_OUT = os.path.join(_HERE, "clinical_quality_weight_fixture.jsonl")
69	
70	# Minimum post-extraction body length to enter the fixture (a too-short body is a fetch stub, a
71	# separate layer's concern — here we want real bodies both classes can be scored on). Named
72	# constant (LAW VI), not a hidden magic number.
73	MIN_BODY_CHARS = 300
74	
75	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	# PRE-REGISTERED OBJECTIVE QUALITY RUBRIC (locked before execution).
77	# Label = 1 (AUTHORITATIVE) / 0 (on-topic-SPAM/shallow) / None (UNDECIDABLE -> excluded from
78	# the proposal so a guess never enters the fixture). Signals are VERIFIABLE DOMAIN IDENTITY +
79	# document-identity markers (DOI / PMID presence, peer-reviewed publisher, gov agency, guideline
80	# body) vs known SEO-spam / social / content-farm / UGC hosts. This is NOT "reads high-quality"
81	# and NOT POLARIS tier — it is registered-domain + document-id identity an auditor can re-check.
82	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
83	# Authoritative registered domains: peer-reviewed publishers, indexers, gov agencies, guideline
84	# bodies, regulator labels. Matched on the registered domain (suffix-aware), document-id-tied.
85	AUTHORITATIVE_DOMAINS = {
86	    # peer-reviewed publishers / indexers
87	    "pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov", "ncbi.nlm.nih.gov", "pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov", "europepmc.org",
88	    "nature.com", "sciencedirect.com", "link.springer.com", "onlinelibrary.wiley.com",
89	    "academic.oup.com", "bmj.com", "bmjopen.bmj.com", "thelancet.com", "jamanetwork.com",
90	    "nejm.org", "cell.com", "frontiersin.org", "mdpi.com", "plos.org", "journals.plos.org",
91	    "tandfonline.com", "sagepub.com", "journals.sagepub.com", "cambridge.org", "karger.com",
92	    "ahajournals.org", "diabetesjournals.org", "aeaweb.org", "pubs.aeaweb.org",
93	    "journals.uchicago.edu", "jmir.org",
94	    # gov agencies / regulators / guideline bodies
95	    "accessdata.fda.gov", "fda.gov", "ema.europa.eu", "europa.eu", "efsa.europa.eu",
96	    "canada.ca", "gc.ca", "nih.gov", "cdc.gov", "who.int", "nice.org.uk", "cochranelibrary.com",
97	    "cochrane.org", "clinicaltrials.gov", "gov.uk",
98	}
99	# DOI resolver — authoritative ONLY when the body/source carries a real DOI/PMID (document
100	# identity), since doi.org resolves to many publishers. Handled specially below.
101	DOI_HOSTS = {"doi.org", "dx.doi.org"}
102	
103	# Known SEO-spam / social / UGC / content-farm hosts: on-topic but NOT authoritative sources.
104	# These are public UGC / video / social / Q&A / content-aggregator hosts — verifiable identity,
105	# not a quality read.
106	SPAM_OR_UGC_DOMAINS = {
107	    "youtube.com", "m.youtube.com", "youtu.be", "facebook.com", "m.facebook.com",
108	    "twitter.com", "x.com", "reddit.com", "old.reddit.com", "quora.com", "medium.com",
109	    "linkedin.com", "pinterest.com", "tiktok.com", "instagram.com", "blogspot.com",
110	    "wordpress.com", "substack.com", "slideshare.net", "scribd.com", "academia.edu",
111	    "researchgate.net",  # UGC author-upload aggregator: on-topic but not the source of record
112	    "wikipedia.org",     # tertiary; on-topic but not a primary authoritative clinical source
113	    "healthline.com", "webmd.com", "verywellhealth.com", "medicalnewstoday.com",
114	}
115	
116	
117	def _registered_domain(host: str) -> str:
118	    """Return a registered-domain-ish key (last two labels, suffix-aware for common 2-part TLDs)."""
119	    host = (host or "").lower().lstrip(".")
120	    if host.startswith("www."):
121	        host = host[4:]
122	    parts = host.split(".")
123	    if len(parts) <= 2:
124	        return host
125	    two_part_tlds = {"co.uk", "org.uk", "gov.uk", "ac.uk", "com.au", "gov.au", "co.jp"}
126	    last2 = ".".join(parts[-2:])
127	    if last2 in two_part_tlds:
128	        return ".".join(parts[-3:])
129	    return last2
130	
131	
132	def _host(url: str) -> str:
133	    try:
134	        return urlparse(url).netloc.lower()
135	    except Exception:
136	        return ""
137	
138	
139	def _domain_matches(host: str, domain_set: set[str]) -> bool:
140	    """True if host == d or host endswith .d for any d in the set."""
141	    host = (host or "").lower().lstrip(".")
142	    if host.startswith("www."):
143	        host = host[4:]
144	    for d in domain_set:
145	        if host == d or host.endswith("." + d):
146	            return True
147	    return False
148	
149	
150	def _source_type(host: str) -> str:
151	    """Coarse source-type bucket for WITHIN-source-type pairing. Identity-based (verifiable
152	    registered domain), NOT a quality read. Used to form (topic x source_type) cells so the AUC
153	    cannot be inflated by a 'journal beats youtube' source-type prior."""
154	    if _domain_matches(host, DOI_HOSTS):
155	        return "doi_resolver"
156	    if _domain_matches(host, {"youtube.com", "youtu.be", "facebook.com", "twitter.com", "x.com",
157	                              "reddit.com", "tiktok.com", "instagram.com", "linkedin.com"}):
158	        return "social_video"
159	    if _domain_matches(host, {"medium.com", "substack.com", "blogspot.com", "wordpress.com",
160	                              "quora.com", "researchgate.net", "academia.edu", "slideshare.net",
161	                              "scribd.com"}):
162	        return "ugc_aggregator"
163	    if _domain_matches(host, {"healthline.com", "webmd.com", "verywellhealth.com",
164	                              "medicalnewstoday.com", "wikipedia.org"}):
165	        return "consumer_health"
166	    # Peer-reviewed article HOSTS that sit under a .gov/.nih.gov suffix (PubMed Central, NCBI,
167	    # PubMed) are article indexers, NOT regulators — match them BEFORE the broad gov_regulator
168	    # set so a `*.nih.gov` PMC article is not mis-bucketed as a regulator. (source_type is an
169	    # identity bucket for within-source-type pairing, never a quality read.)
170	    if _domain_matches(host, {"pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov", "ncbi.nlm.nih.gov",
171	                              "pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov", "europepmc.org"}):
172	        return "peer_reviewed_publisher"
173	    if _domain_matches(host, {"fda.gov", "accessdata.fda.gov", "ema.europa.eu", "europa.eu",
174	                              "canada.ca", "gc.ca", "nih.gov", "cdc.gov", "who.int",
175	                              "clinicaltrials.gov", "nice.org.uk", "gov.uk"}):
176	        return "gov_regulator"
177	    if _domain_matches(host, AUTHORITATIVE_DOMAINS):
178	        return "peer_reviewed_publisher"
179	    return "other_web"
180	
181	
182	def objective_rubric_label(*, url: str, doi: str | None, pmid: str | None) -> tuple[int | None, str]:
183	    """PRE-REGISTERED objective rubric. Returns (label, reason).
184	
185	    label = 1 authoritative iff (a) the registered domain is a peer-reviewed publisher / gov /
186	    guideline body, OR (b) the source carries a real DOI or PMID (document-of-record identity)
187	    AND is not a known UGC/social host. label = 0 iff the host is a known SEO-spam / social /
188	    UGC / consumer-health-farm domain. label = None (UNDECIDABLE -> excluded) otherwise — we
189	    NEVER guess a label into the fixture.
190	    """
191	    host = _host(url)
192	    has_doc_id = bool((doi or "").strip()) or bool((pmid or "").strip())
193	
194	    if _domain_matches(host, SPAM_OR_UGC_DOMAINS):
195	        return 0, f"known_ugc_or_spam_domain:{_registered_domain(host)}"
196	    if _domain_matches(host, AUTHORITATIVE_DOMAINS):
197	        return 1, f"authoritative_domain:{_registered_domain(host)}"
198	    if _domain_matches(host, DOI_HOSTS) and has_doc_id:
199	        return 1, "doi_resolver_with_document_id"
200	    if has_doc_id:
201	        # carries a DOI/PMID but on an unlisted host: document-of-record identity present, not
202	        # a known UGC host -> authoritative by document identity (verifiable, auditable).
203	        return 1, "carries_doi_or_pmid_document_id"
204	    return None, f"undecidable_unlisted_domain:{_registered_domain(host)}"
205	
206	
207	def _body_text(ev: dict) -> str:
208	    """Post-extraction body span for a source. The banked snapshot stores the cleaned body as
209	    `direct_quote` (verified: up to 25000 chars, the post-extraction/clean_fetch_body output)."""
210	    return (ev.get("direct_quote") or "").strip()
211	
212	
213	def _on_topic_ids(rows: list[dict], slug: str) -> set[str]:
214	    """Evidence ids confirmed ON-TOPIC for the question via the reused LABEL_SETS seam (POS side
215	    only — clearly on-topic). Both fixture classes must be on-topic; off-topic rows are dropped
216	    so we measure quality, not topic."""
217	    if not LABEL_SETS or label_rows is None or slug not in LABEL_SETS:
218	        # seam unavailable: keep all rows (the rubric still labels quality); flagged in manifest.
219	        return {ev.get("evidence_id") for ev in rows if ev.get("evidence_id")}
220	    pos, _neg = label_rows(rows, LABEL_SETS[slug])
221	    return {ev.get("evidence_id") for ev in pos if ev.get("evidence_id")}
222	
223	
224	def build_rows_from_snapshot(snapshot: dict, slug: str) -> list[dict]:
225	    """Build proposed fixture rows from one loaded corpus_snapshot dict."""
226	    evidence = snapshot.get("evidence_for_gen") or snapshot.get("evidence") or []
227	    on_topic = _on_topic_ids(evidence, slug)
228	    rows: list[dict] = []
229	    for ev in evidence:
230	        ev_id = ev.get("evidence_id")
231	        url = ev.get("source_url") or ev.get("url") or ""
232	        body = _body_text(ev)
233	        if len(body) < MIN_BODY_CHARS:
234	            continue
235	        if on_topic and ev_id not in on_topic:
236	            continue  # keep both classes on-topic
237	        label, reason = objective_rubric_label(
238	            url=url, doi=ev.get("doi"), pmid=ev.get("pmid")
239	        )
240	        if label is None:
241	            continue  # undecidable -> never guessed into the fixture
242	        host = _host(url)
243	        rows.append({
244	            "source_id": f"{slug}:{ev_id}",
245	            "topic_id": slug,
246	            "url": url,
247	            "registered_domain": _registered_domain(host),
248	            "source_type": _source_type(host),
249	            "post_extraction_body": body,
250	            "body_sha256": hashlib.sha256(body.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest(),
251	            # ---- label provenance (the Codex iter-1 P1 contract) ----
252	            "rubric_label": label,
253	            "rubric_reason": reason,
254	            "family_a_label": label,            # deterministic objective rubric = family A proposal
255	            "family_b_label": None,             # Codex family adjudication (out-of-band)
256	            "operator_spotcheck": None,         # operator stratified spot-check (out-of-band)
257	            "label_status": "proposed",         # -> "adjudicated" only after two-family + spotcheck
258	            "scored_label": None,               # set ONLY when label_status == "adjudicated"
259	            "label_independent_of_tier": True,  # rubric never reads POLARIS tier/authority_score
260	        })
261	    return rows
262	
263	
264	def load_snapshot(snapshot_root: str, slug: str) -> dict | None:
265	    path = os.path.join(snapshot_root, slug, "corpus_snapshot.json")
266	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
267	        return None
268	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
269	        return json.load(fh)
270	
271	
272	def build_fixture(*, snapshot_root: str, slugs: list[str]) -> tuple[list[dict], dict]:
273	    """Build all proposed rows + a manifest. Returns (rows, manifest)."""
274	    all_rows: list[dict] = []
275	    per_slug = {}
276	    missing = []
277	    for slug in slugs:
278	        snap = load_snapshot(snapshot_root, slug)
279	        if snap is None:
280	            missing.append(slug)
281	            continue
282	        rows = build_rows_from_snapshot(snap, slug)
283	        per_slug[slug] = {
284	            "n_rows": len(rows),
285	            "n_authoritative": sum(1 for r in rows if r["rubric_label"] == 1),
286	            "n_spam": sum(1 for r in rows if r["rubric_label"] == 0),
287	        }
288	        all_rows.extend(rows)
289	
290	    n_auth = sum(1 for r in all_rows if r["rubric_label"] == 1)
291	    n_spam = sum(1 for r in all_rows if r["rubric_label"] == 0)
292	    fixture_sha = hashlib.sha256(
293	        json.dumps([r["source_id"] for r in all_rows], sort_keys=True).encode("utf-8")
294	    ).hexdigest()
295	    source_types = sorted({r["source_type"] for r in all_rows})
296	    manifest = {
297	        "fixture": "clinical_quality_weight_fixture.jsonl",
298	        "n_rows": len(all_rows),
299	        "n_authoritative": n_auth,
300	        "n_spam": n_spam,
301	        "n_topics": len({r["topic_id"] for r in all_rows}),
302	        "source_types": source_types,
303	        "per_slug": per_slug,
304	        "missing_slugs": missing,
305	        "min_body_chars": MIN_BODY_CHARS,
306	        "label_status": "proposed",
307	        "fixture_id_sha256": fixture_sha,
308	        "label_integrity": {
309	            "scored_label_source": "pre-registered objective rubric (verifiable domain/document "
310	                                    "identity) -> family-A proposal; NEVER reads/quality, NEVER "
311	                                    "POLARIS tier/authority_score",
312	            "family_a": "deterministic objective rubric (this script)",
313	            "family_b": "Codex adjudication — OUT OF BAND, pending (label_status stays 'proposed')",
314	            "operator_spotcheck": "stratified sample spot-check — OUT OF BAND, pending",
315	            "judge_role": "PROPOSE only; never sets the scored label",
316	            "llm_judge_candidate_no_self_grade": "GLM-5.2 LLM-judge candidate is scored ONLY vs "
317	                                                 "a different family's labels (no circularity)",
318	        },
319	        "adjudication_blocker": (
320	            "label_status == 'proposed' for all rows: two-family (Codex) adjudication + operator "
321	            "stratified spot-check are out-of-band and PENDING. run_bakeoff scores only "
322	            "'adjudicated' rows; until adjudication, results are flagged provisional, never "
323	            "silently treated as final."
324	        ),
325	    }
326	    return all_rows, manifest
327	
328	
329	def write_fixture(rows: list[dict], out_path: str) -> None:
330	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)
331	    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
332	        for r in rows:
333	            fh.write(json.dumps(r, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
334	
335	
336	def load_fixture(path: str) -> list[dict]:
337	    """Load a built fixture jsonl. Fail loud if missing (never a silent empty)."""
338	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
339	        raise FileNotFoundError(
340	            f"quality_weight fixture not found: {path} — run build_fixture.py first "
341	            f"(it builds from banked corpus_snapshot.json bodies)."
342	        )
343	    rows = []
344	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
345	        for line in fh:
346	            line = line.strip()
347	            if line:
348	                rows.append(json.loads(line))
349	    return rows
350	
351	
352	def main(argv=None) -> int:
353	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
354	    ap.add_argument("--snapshot-root", default=DEFAULT_SNAPSHOT_ROOT)
355	    ap.add_argument("--slugs", default=",".join(DEFAULT_SLUGS))
356	    ap.add_argument("--out", default=DEFAULT_OUT)
357	    ap.add_argument("--manifest", default=os.path.join(_HERE, "clinical_quality_weight_manifest.json"))
358	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
359	
360	    slugs = [s for s in args.slugs.split(",") if s.strip()]
361	    rows, manifest = build_fixture(snapshot_root=args.snapshot_root, slugs=slugs)
362	    if not rows:
363	        print(
364	            f"FAIL LOUD: built 0 fixture rows from {args.snapshot_root} (slugs={slugs}). "
365	            f"Check the snapshot root exists and contains corpus_snapshot.json files.",
366	            file=sys.stderr,
367	        )
368	        return 1
369	    write_fixture(rows, args.out)
370	    with open(args.manifest, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
371	        json.dump(manifest, fh, indent=2)
372	    print(f"built {manifest['n_rows']} rows "
373	          f"({manifest['n_authoritative']} authoritative / {manifest['n_spam']} spam) "
374	          f"across {manifest['n_topics']} topics, {len(manifest['source_types'])} source-types")
375	    print(f"  source_types: {manifest['source_types']}")
376	    print(f"  -> {args.out}")
377	    print(f"  -> {args.manifest}")
378	    print(f"  label_status=proposed (BLOCKER: {manifest['adjudication_blocker']})")
379	    return 0
380	
381	
382	if __name__ == "__main__":
383	    raise SystemExit(main())
384

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""GATE-0 validity harness for the quality_weight retrieval-isolation bake-off (I-ret-002 #1294).
3	
4	This is the anti-drb_72 gate. NO candidate AUC is trusted until BOTH halves are green:
5	
6	  (A) SCORER-MATH canary — feed the within-cell AUC scorer (label, score) sets whose answers
7	      are mathematically KNOWN: perfectly-ranked -> EXACTLY 1.0; perfectly-inverted -> EXACTLY
8	      0.0; all-tied/constant -> EXACTLY 0.5; a seeded-random set -> within a pre-registered
9	      tolerance band of 0.5. If any deviates, the metric is mis-wired (the drb_72 "fake numbers
10	      that look real" failure) and no candidate is believed.
11	
12	  (B) PER-CANDIDATE LIVENESS canary — for each REAL classifier candidate, feed one obviously
13	      authoritative doc and one obvious-garbage doc and assert the candidate ranks authoritative
14	      STRICTLY above garbage AND that the two scores DIFFER (not a constant). A candidate that
15	      returns a stub / empty / constant / load-fails / has a missing key FAILS LOUD (non-zero
16	      exit), never scores a believable-low number. The NEGATIVE CONTROLS (constant / random) are
17	      EXEMPT from semantic-direction liveness (they are EXPECTED to land near 0.5) — the harness
18	      instead asserts they DO land near 0.5 as a separate scorer sanity check.
19	
20	The metric primitives (within-(topic x source_type)-cell paired AUC) live here so run_bakeoff.py
21	and smoke_test.py import the SAME scorer the canary validates — there is exactly one scorer.
22	
23	Design rules honored:
24	  - §-1.1: AUC is rank separation of a scalar weight vs a LABELED binary ground truth — never a
25	    count / pattern-presence / metadata proxy. Pairs are formed ONLY within a (topic x
26	    source_type) cell so a global "journal=high, youtube=low" source-type prior cannot inflate
27	    the score (that prior is exactly the proxy this layer is paid to kill).
28	  - §-1.3 weight-not-filter: the weight is never thresholded to drop a source anywhere here.
29	  - Faithfulness engine untouched: this harness only ranks scalar weights; it never invokes,
30	    reads, or relaxes strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance.
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import random
36	import sys
37	from collections import defaultdict
38	from typing import Callable, Iterable, Sequence
39	
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	# Pre-registered numeric constants (locked before execution; no magic numbers
42	# scattered through the logic). LAW VI: tests/thresholds are named constants.
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	AUC_PERFECT = 1.0
45	AUC_INVERTED = 0.0
46	AUC_CONSTANT = 0.5
47	# Random-control band: a seeded uniform-random scorer over a finite fixture will not land EXACTLY
48	# at 0.5; it must land within this half-width of 0.5. 0.5 (the chance level) is the centre; the
49	# band is wide enough that a correctly-wired random scorer reliably passes on the synthetic canary
50	# set, narrow enough that a broken scorer (e.g. one that secretly tracks the label) fails it.
51	RANDOM_BAND_HALF_WIDTH = 0.15
52	# Liveness: the authoritative-vs-garbage score gap a real classifier must exceed (in raw score
53	# units AFTER the scorer's own scale — we only require strict ordering + a non-trivial gap so a
54	# float-jitter "constant" cannot sneak through as "barely higher").
55	LIVENESS_MIN_SCORE_GAP = 1e-6
56	
57	
58	class GateZeroQualityError(RuntimeError):
59	    """Raised fail-loud when a GATE-0 validity check fails. A run that raises this is INVALID
60	    and MUST be excluded from any score. Structural guard against the drb_72 trust-garbage
61	    failure: a stub / constant / load-failed / keyless candidate raises here, it never scores."""
62	
63	
64	# ===========================================================================
65	# METRIC PRIMITIVES (the ONE scorer the canary validates)
66	# ===========================================================================
67	def auc_pos_gt_neg(pos_scores: Sequence[float], neg_scores: Sequence[float]) -> float | None:
68	    """ROC-AUC computed as P(score(pos) > score(neg)) over all POS x NEG pairs (ties = 0.5).
69	
70	    This pairwise form is mathematically identical to ROC-AUC and needs no sklearn dependency.
71	    Reused verbatim from scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py (the named LABEL_SETS seam's AUC).
72	    Returns None when either class is empty (no pairs => undefined, never a fake 0.5).
73	    """
74	    if not pos_scores or not neg_scores:
75	        return None
76	    wins = ties = 0
77	    for p in pos_scores:
78	        for n in neg_scores:
79	            if p > n:
80	                wins += 1
81	            elif p == n:
82	                ties += 1
83	    return (wins + 0.5 * ties) / (len(pos_scores) * len(neg_scores))
84	
85	
86	def paired_within_cell_auc(rows: Iterable[dict], score_key: str = "score") -> dict:
87	    """Micro-averaged ROC-AUC with POS/NEG pairs formed ONLY within a (topic_id x source_type)
88	    cell.
89	
90	    Each row must carry: ``label`` (1 = authoritative, 0 = on-topic-spam), ``topic_id``,
91	    ``source_type``, and ``score_key`` (the candidate's scalar weight). Pairs cross only a
92	    same-cell authoritative vs same-cell spam item, so the metric measures QUALITY separation,
93	    never a topic or source-type prior (the §-1.1 proxy). We MICRO-average (sum all cells' wins
94	    and ties, divide by all cells' pair count) rather than macro-average per-cell AUCs, so a
95	    1-pair cell cannot flake the headline number (advisor pt 3).
96	
97	    Returns {auc, n_pairs, n_cells_scored, n_cells_skipped, per_cell:[...]}. auc is None when no
98	    cell has both a POS and a NEG item (fail-loud upstream, never a fake number).
99	    """
100	    cells: dict[tuple, dict] = defaultdict(lambda: {"pos": [], "neg": []})
101	    for r in rows:
102	        label = r.get("label")
103	        if label not in (0, 1):
104	            raise GateZeroQualityError(
105	                f"row label must be 0 or 1 (got {label!r}); the scored label is the binary "
106	                f"authoritative/spam ground truth, never a tier or a continuous proxy"
107	            )
108	        key = (r.get("topic_id"), r.get("source_type"))
109	        cells[key]["pos" if label == 1 else "neg"].append(float(r[score_key]))
110	
111	    total_wins = total_ties = total_pairs = 0
112	    per_cell = []
113	    n_skipped = 0
114	    for (topic_id, source_type), bucket in sorted(cells.items(), key=lambda kv: str(kv[0])):
115	        pos, neg = bucket["pos"], bucket["neg"]
116	        if not pos or not neg:
117	            n_skipped += 1
118	            continue
119	        wins = ties = 0
120	        for p in pos:
121	            for n in neg:
122	                if p > n:
123	                    wins += 1
124	                elif p == n:
125	                    ties += 1
126	        npairs = len(pos) * len(neg)
127	        total_wins += wins
128	        total_ties += ties
129	        total_pairs += npairs
130	        per_cell.append({
131	            "topic_id": topic_id,
132	            "source_type": source_type,
133	            "n_pos": len(pos),
134	            "n_neg": len(neg),
135	            "n_pairs": npairs,
136	            "cell_auc": round((wins + 0.5 * ties) / npairs, 6),
137	        })
138	
139	    auc = None if total_pairs == 0 else (total_wins + 0.5 * total_ties) / total_pairs
140	    return {
141	        "auc": None if auc is None else round(auc, 6),
142	        "n_pairs": total_pairs,
143	        "n_cells_scored": len(per_cell),
144	        "n_cells_skipped": n_skipped,
145	        "per_cell": per_cell,
146	    }
147	
148	
149	# ===========================================================================
150	# GATE-0 HALF (A): SCORER-MATH CANARY
151	# ===========================================================================
152	def _rows(scores: Sequence[float], labels: Sequence[int], topic_id="t", source_type="s") -> list:
153	    return [
154	        {"score": s, "label": l, "topic_id": topic_id, "source_type": source_type}
155	        for s, l in zip(scores, labels)
156	    ]
157	
158	
159	def run_scorer_math_canary(*, seed: int = 1234) -> dict:
160	    """Validate the within-cell AUC scorer on KNOWN-answer (label, score) sets. Returns a report
161	    dict; raises GateZeroQualityError fail-loud on ANY deviation (the drb_72 guard)."""
162	    report: dict = {"checks": []}
163	
164	    def _record(name: str, got, expected, ok: bool):
165	        report["checks"].append({"name": name, "got": got, "expected": expected, "ok": ok})
166	        if not ok:
167	            raise GateZeroQualityError(
168	                f"SCORER-MATH canary FAILED on {name!r}: got {got!r}, expected {expected!r}. "
169	                f"The AUC scorer is mis-wired; no candidate score is trusted (drb_72 guard)."
170	            )
171	
172	    # perfect: all authoritative (label 1) score above all spam (label 0) within one cell -> 1.0
173	    perfect = paired_within_cell_auc(_rows([0.9, 0.8, 0.7, 0.2, 0.1], [1, 1, 1, 0, 0]))
174	    _record("perfect_ranking", perfect["auc"], AUC_PERFECT, perfect["auc"] == AUC_PERFECT)
175	
176	    # inverted: every authoritative scores BELOW every spam -> 0.0 (proves no fake credit)
177	    inverted = paired_within_cell_auc(_rows([0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.8, 0.9], [1, 1, 1, 0, 0]))
178	    _record("inverted_ranking", inverted["auc"], AUC_INVERTED, inverted["auc"] == AUC_INVERTED)
179	
180	    # constant: every score identical -> all ties -> EXACTLY 0.5
181	    const = paired_within_cell_auc(_rows([0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5], [1, 1, 1, 0, 0, 0]))
182	    _record("constant_scores", const["auc"], AUC_CONSTANT, const["auc"] == AUC_CONSTANT)
183	
184	    # random control: seeded uniform scores, balanced labels -> within band of 0.5
185	    rng = random.Random(seed)
186	    n = 400
187	    labels = [1] * (n // 2) + [0] * (n // 2)
188	    rscores = [rng.random() for _ in range(n)]
189	    rnd = paired_within_cell_auc(_rows(rscores, labels))
190	    in_band = abs(rnd["auc"] - AUC_CONSTANT) <= RANDOM_BAND_HALF_WIDTH
191	    _record(
192	        "random_band",
193	        rnd["auc"],
194	        f"{AUC_CONSTANT}+/-{RANDOM_BAND_HALF_WIDTH}",
195	        in_band,
196	    )
197	
198	    # cross-cell isolation: a per-source-type prior that is RIGHT globally but provides ZERO
199	    # within-cell separation must NOT score above 0.5. Two topics; within each topic the spam
200	    # item scores HIGHER than the authoritative item (the score tracks source-type, not quality).
201	    # Global AUC would be misled; within-cell AUC must report <= 0.5.
202	    prior_rows = [
203	        {"score": 0.2, "label": 1, "topic_id": "tA", "source_type": "x"},
204	        {"score": 0.9, "label": 0, "topic_id": "tA", "source_type": "x"},
205	        {"score": 0.2, "label": 1, "topic_id": "tB", "source_type": "x"},
206	        {"score": 0.9, "label": 0, "topic_id": "tB", "source_type": "x"},
207	    ]
208	    prior = paired_within_cell_auc(prior_rows)
209	    _record(
210	        "within_cell_kills_sourcetype_prior",
211	        prior["auc"],
212	        "<=0.5",
213	        prior["auc"] is not None and prior["auc"] <= AUC_CONSTANT,
214	    )
215	
216	    report["ok"] = True
217	    return report
218	
219	
220	# ===========================================================================
221	# GATE-0 HALF (B): PER-CANDIDATE LIVENESS CANARY
222	# ===========================================================================
223	def assert_candidate_live(
224	    candidate_name: str,
225	    scorer: Callable[[str], float],
226	    authoritative_doc: str,
227	    garbage_doc: str,
228	) -> dict:
229	    """Liveness gate for ONE real classifier. The scorer must (a) load + score without raising,
230	    (b) rank the authoritative doc STRICTLY above the garbage doc, and (c) emit two scores that
231	    DIFFER by more than LIVENESS_MIN_SCORE_GAP (so a constant/stub returning the same number for
232	    both cannot pass). Any failure raises GateZeroQualityError fail-loud.
233	
234	    A load-fail, a missing API key, or an empty/None return propagates as a raise here — NEVER a
235	    believable-low score. This is the precise drb_72 anti-pattern the layer must defeat.
236	    """
237	    try:
238	        s_auth = scorer(authoritative_doc)
239	        s_junk = scorer(garbage_doc)
240	    except Exception as exc:  # load-fail / missing-key / runtime error -> fail loud, never score
241	        raise GateZeroQualityError(
242	            f"LIVENESS canary: candidate {candidate_name!r} raised while scoring "
243	            f"({type(exc).__name__}: {exc}). A load-fail / missing-key / stub candidate FAILS "
244	            f"LOUD here; it is never given a believable-low AUC (drb_72 guard)."
245	        ) from exc
246	
247	    if s_auth is None or s_junk is None:
248	        raise GateZeroQualityError(
249	            f"LIVENESS canary: candidate {candidate_name!r} returned None "
250	            f"(auth={s_auth!r} junk={s_junk!r}); a stub/empty return FAILS LOUD, never scores."
251	        )
252	    s_auth = float(s_auth)
253	    s_junk = float(s_junk)
254	    gap = s_auth - s_junk
255	    if abs(s_auth - s_junk) <= LIVENESS_MIN_SCORE_GAP:
256	        raise GateZeroQualityError(
257	            f"LIVENESS canary: candidate {candidate_name!r} returned a CONSTANT/degenerate "
258	            f"signal (auth={s_auth} junk={s_junk}, |gap|={abs(gap):.3e} <= "
259	            f"{LIVENESS_MIN_SCORE_GAP:.3e}). A classifier that does not vary cannot be trusted "
260	            f"to rank quality — it would still produce a real-looking AUC. FAIL LOUD."
261	        )
262	    if gap <= 0:
263	        raise GateZeroQualityError(
264	            f"LIVENESS canary: candidate {candidate_name!r} ranked GARBAGE >= AUTHORITATIVE "
265	            f"(auth={s_auth} junk={s_junk}). A real quality classifier MUST score the FDA/Cochrane "
266	            f"body above keyword-stuffed cookie-banner spam. FAIL LOUD (wrong-direction / mis-wired)."
267	        )
268	    return {"candidate": candidate_name, "score_auth": s_auth, "score_junk": s_junk,
269	            "gap": gap, "ok": True}
270	
271	
272	def assert_control_near_half(control_name: str, auc: float | None) -> dict:
273	    """Negative-control sanity: constant / random controls are EXEMPT from semantic-direction
274	    liveness but MUST land near 0.5 (no quality signal). A control that lands FAR from 0.5 means
275	    the harness leaked the label into the control — fail loud."""
276	    if auc is None:
277	        raise GateZeroQualityError(
278	            f"control {control_name!r} produced AUC=None (no scorable pairs); the control fixture "
279	            f"is degenerate. FAIL LOUD."
280	        )
281	    if abs(auc - AUC_CONSTANT) > RANDOM_BAND_HALF_WIDTH:
282	        raise GateZeroQualityError(
283	            f"control {control_name!r} AUC={auc:.4f} is OUTSIDE 0.5+/-{RANDOM_BAND_HALF_WIDTH}. "
284	            f"A no-signal control must land near chance; landing far means the harness leaked the "
285	            f"label into the control scorer (drb_72 guard). FAIL LOUD."
286	        )
287	    return {"control": control_name, "auc": auc, "ok": True}
288	
289	
290	def main(argv: Sequence[str] | None = None) -> int:
291	    """CLI: run the scorer-math canary standalone. The per-candidate liveness canary needs the
292	    real loaded scorers, so it is driven from run_bakeoff.py / smoke_test.py with the candidate
293	    registry; this entry point validates the metric math (half A) on its own."""
294	    try:
295	        report = run_scorer_math_canary()
296	    except GateZeroQualityError as exc:
297	        print(f"GATE-0 scorer-math canary: FAIL\n  {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
298	        return 1
299	    for chk in report["checks"]:
300	        print(f"  [{'PASS' if chk['ok'] else 'FAIL'}] {chk['name']}: "
301	              f"got={chk['got']} expected={chk['expected']}")
302	    print("GATE-0 scorer-math canary: PASS (metric math validated)")
303	    return 0
304	
305	
306	if __name__ == "__main__":
307	    raise SystemExit(main())
308
