1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Run the quality_weight retrieval-isolation bake-off (I-ret-002 #1294, §4).
3	
4	For each candidate quality-weight model: load it by its EXACT HF/pip/API id, score every
5	ADJUDICATED fixture source -> a scalar weight, compute the within-(topic x source_type)-cell
6	ROC-AUC of that weight vs the binary label (gate0.paired_within_cell_auc), and write a ranked
7	results JSON.
8	
9	GATE-0 IS MANDATORY and runs FIRST (gate0.run_scorer_math_canary + per-candidate liveness via
10	gate0.assert_candidate_live). No candidate AUC is emitted until the scorer-math canary is green
11	AND the candidate passed its own liveness check. A stub / constant / load-failed / missing-key
12	candidate FAILS LOUD and is recorded as non-functional — it is NEVER given a believable-low AUC.
13	
14	HONEST FLAGS (never faked):
15	  * runnable == "no_key"   -> registered but SKIPPED (recorded, not scored). E.g. GLM-5.2 judge.
16	  * runnable == "needs_gpu" -> gated behind a real runtime check (torch.cuda / fasttext import);
17	    if the runtime is absent the candidate is recorded as skipped-needs-gpu, never faked.
18	  * runnable == "yes"      -> CPU; runs anywhere.
19	
20	NEGATIVE CONTROLS (constant / random): NOT candidates, EXEMPT from the semantic-direction
21	liveness canary; the harness instead asserts they land near 0.5 (gate0.assert_control_near_half).
22	
23	WEIGHT-NOT-FILTER (§-1.3): the scalar weight is NEVER thresholded to drop a source here. The
24	metric (ROC-AUC) integrates over all thresholds — it is the formal statement of weight-not-filter.
25	
26	FAITHFULNESS untouched: every candidate is a SCORER (no span is rewritten); strict_verify / NLI /
27	4-role / provenance are never invoked.
28	
29	The candidate loaders are written as `load() -> scorer_callable`. Heavy model loads (fastText /
30	transformers / torch / API) are imported INSIDE load() so smoke_test.py can monkeypatch load()
31	with a synthetic scorer — NO GPU, NO network, NO download in the smoke.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import argparse
37	import json
38	import os
39	import sys
40	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
41	from typing import Callable, Optional
42	
43	_HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
44	# repo root = .../scripts/dr_benchmark/retrieval_bakeoff/quality_weight -> up 4 (so `from src...`
45	# resolves regardless of cwd, e.g. the POLARIS heuristic baseline loader).
46	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(_HERE, "..", "..", "..", ".."))
47	for _p in (_HERE, _REPO_ROOT):
48	    if _p not in sys.path:
49	        sys.path.insert(0, _p)
50	
51	import gate0  # noqa: E402  (sibling module in the same layer dir)
52	import build_fixture  # noqa: E402
53	
54	# Family of the label-adjudication producers; the LLM-judge candidate must NOT be in this set
55	# (no-self-grade guard, advisor pt 2). Labels are produced by Claude + Codex families.
56	LABEL_ADJUDICATION_FAMILIES = frozenset({"claude", "codex"})
57	
58	# Canary docs for the per-candidate liveness gate (obvious authoritative vs obvious garbage).
59	# Real, hand-written extremes — an FDA/Cochrane-style clinical body vs keyword-stuffed cookie spam.
60	_AUTHORITATIVE_CANARY_DOC = (
61	    "FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION. INDICATIONS AND USAGE. This randomized, double-blind, "
62	    "placebo-controlled trial enrolled 1,248 adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus. The primary "
63	    "endpoint was the change in HbA1c from baseline at 24 weeks. Treatment reduced HbA1c by 1.2 "
64	    "percentage points (95% confidence interval 1.0 to 1.4, p<0.001) versus placebo. Adverse "
65	    "events were consistent with the known safety profile. CONTRAINDICATIONS: known "
66	    "hypersensitivity to the active ingredient. DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION: the recommended "
67	    "starting dose is 5 mg once daily, titrated based on tolerability and glycemic response."
68	)
69	_GARBAGE_CANARY_DOC = (
70	    "Accept all cookies. Subscribe now for our newsletter! BEST cheap pills online buy now "
71	    "discount discount discount. cookie policy privacy policy terms of service all rights "
72	    "reserved. Click here click here click here. weight loss miracle cure buy cheap meds no "
73	    "prescription needed share this article sign up sign up sign up best deals best deals."
74	)
75	
76	
77	@dataclass
78	class Candidate:
79	    name: str
80	    impl_id: str
81	    license: str
82	    runnable: str            # "yes" | "no_key" | "needs_gpu"
83	    role: str                # "baseline" | "candidate" | "yardstick_non_sovereign" | "control"
84	    family: str              # model lineage tag (for the no-self-grade guard)
85	    loader: Optional[Callable[[], Callable[[str], float]]] = None  # load() -> scorer(text)->float
86	    notes: str = ""
87	    flags: list = field(default_factory=list)
88	
89	
90	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
91	# Candidate loaders. Each returns scorer(text)->float. Heavy imports INSIDE the
92	# loader so smoke can monkeypatch without importing torch/fasttext.
93	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
94	def _load_polaris_heuristic() -> Callable[[str], float]:
95	    """Baseline: POLARIS heuristic score_content_quality used as a RANKER (the 0..1 score is the
96	    weight), NOT as the document-gate drop it ships as (the gate stays default-OFF)."""
97	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.content_quality_gate import score_content_quality
98	
99	    def scorer(text: str) -> float:
100	        score, _reasons = score_content_quality(text)
101	        return float(score)
102	
103	    return scorer
104	
105	
106	def _load_dclm_fasttext() -> Callable[[str], float]:
107	    """DCLM OH-2.5+ELI5 fastText (mlfoundations/fasttext-oh-eli5). P(__label__hq) = the weight."""
108	    import fasttext  # noqa
109	    from huggingface_hub import hf_hub_download
110	
111	    # resolve the .bin via the hub (exact filename varies; pick the single .bin in the repo).
112	    from huggingface_hub import list_repo_files
113	    files = list_repo_files("mlfoundations/fasttext-oh-eli5")
114	    bins = [f for f in files if f.endswith(".bin")]
115	    if not bins:
116	        raise RuntimeError("no .bin in mlfoundations/fasttext-oh-eli5")
117	    model_path = hf_hub_download("mlfoundations/fasttext-oh-eli5", bins[0])
118	    model = fasttext.load_model(model_path)
119	
120	    def scorer(text: str) -> float:
121	        t = " ".join(text.split())[:100000]
122	        labels, probs = model.predict(t, k=2)
123	        d = {lab: float(p) for lab, p in zip(labels, probs)}
124	        return d.get("__label__hq", 0.0)
125	
126	    return scorer
127	
128	
129	def _load_ultra_fineweb() -> Callable[[str], float]:
130	    """Ultra-FineWeb verification-loop fastText (openbmb/Ultra-FineWeb-classifier). Pred score of
131	    the high-quality label = the weight."""
132	    import fasttext  # noqa
133	    from huggingface_hub import hf_hub_download, list_repo_files
134	    files = list_repo_files("openbmb/Ultra-FineWeb-classifier")
135	    bins = [f for f in files if f.endswith(".bin")]
136	    if not bins:
137	        raise RuntimeError("no .bin in openbmb/Ultra-FineWeb-classifier")
138	    model_path = hf_hub_download("openbmb/Ultra-FineWeb-classifier", bins[0])
139	    model = fasttext.load_model(model_path)
140	
141	    def scorer(text: str) -> float:
142	        t = " ".join(text.split())[:100000]
143	        labels, probs = model.predict(t, k=2)
144	        # high-quality label is the positive class; pick the max-prob positive-ish label
145	        d = {lab: float(p) for lab, p in zip(labels, probs)}
146	        for pos in ("__label__1", "__label__hq", "__label__pos"):
147	            if pos in d:
148	                return d[pos]
149	        # fallback: prob of the lexicographically-last label (positive convention varies)
150	        return d.get(sorted(d)[-1], 0.0) if d else 0.0
151	
152	    return scorer
153	
154	
155	def _load_fineweb_edu() -> Callable[[str], float]:
156	    """FineWeb-Edu regression classifier (HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu-classifier). 0..5 educational
157	    score = the weight. Educational value is a quality PROXY, not clinical authority (honest)."""
158	    import torch  # noqa
159	    from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForSequenceClassification
160	    mid = "HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu-classifier"
161	    tok = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(mid)
162	    model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(mid)
163	    model.eval()
164	
165	    def scorer(text: str) -> float:
166	        inp = tok(text, return_tensors="pt", truncation=True, max_length=512)
167	        with torch.no_grad():
168	            logits = model(**inp).logits
169	        return float(logits.squeeze(-1).item())
170	
171	    return scorer
172	
173	
174	def _load_nemotron_cc() -> Callable[[str], float]:
175	    """Nemotron-CC ensemble: mean of the two NVIDIA arctic-embed edu heads (nemotron-4 + mixtral),
176	    each 0..5, mean-of-normalized per the Nemotron-CC paper. (DCLM fastText fusion is folded in by
177	    its own candidate; here we score the two NVIDIA heads' mean as the ensemble weight.)"""
178	    import torch  # noqa
179	    from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForSequenceClassification
180	    ids = [
181	        "nvidia/nemocurator-fineweb-nemotron-4-edu-classifier",
182	        "nvidia/nemocurator-fineweb-mixtral-edu-classifier",
183	    ]
184	    heads = []
185	    for mid in ids:
186	        tok = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(mid)
187	        model = AutoModelForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(mid)
188	        model.eval()
189	        heads.append((tok, model))
190	
191	    def scorer(text: str) -> float:
192	        vals = []
193	        for tok, model in heads:
194	            inp = tok(text, return_tensors="pt", truncation=True, max_length=512)
195	            with torch.no_grad():
196	                logits = model(**inp).logits
197	            vals.append(float(logits.squeeze(-1).item()) / 5.0)  # normalize 0..5 -> 0..1
198	        return sum(vals) / len(vals)
199	
200	    return scorer
201	
202	
203	def _load_essential_web() -> Callable[[str], float]:
204	    """Essential-Web EAI-Distill-0.5b taxonomy SLM (EssentialAI/eai-taxonomy-0.5b). Use the
205	    reasoning-depth / technical-correctness ordinal axis as the weight — NOT the topic (FDC) field
206	    (that would leak relevance). License under-specified on card (401) -> VERIFY-BEFORE-ADOPT.
207	
208	    The taxonomy SLM emits a 12-field STRUCTURED label, not a scalar; the exact reasoning-depth
209	    field decode/parse must be finalized against the model card (pending the 401 resolution).
210	    Until then this loader FAILS LOUD AT LOAD TIME (status -> load_failed) rather than (a) wasting
211	    a 0.5B download to then liveness-fail, or (b) returning a fake number. Wire the parse before
212	    enabling this candidate. NOT wired yet — registered so it is never silently dropped."""
213	    raise RuntimeError(
214	        "eai-taxonomy-0.5b NOT wired: 12-field taxonomy reasoning-depth parse not finalized "
215	        "(card 401, VERIFY-BEFORE-ADOPT). Wire the structured-field decode before scoring; fail "
216	        "loud at load time instead of downloading a model only to return a fake/raising weight."
217	    )
218	
219	
220	def _load_glm_judge() -> Callable[[str], float]:
221	    """GLM-5.2 LLM-judge yardstick (OpenRouter z-ai/glm-5.1). no_key: registered but skipped here
222	    (informative ceiling only; an LLM-in-extraction-loop is a faithfulness hazard => never a
223	    production pick). Scored ONLY vs a DIFFERENT family's labels (no self-grading)."""
224	    raise RuntimeError("GLM-5.2 judge requires an API key (no_key) — registered but not run here.")
225	
226	
227	def build_registry() -> list[Candidate]:
228	    return [
229	        Candidate(
230	            name="polaris_heuristic",
231	            impl_id="src/polaris_graph/retrieval/content_quality_gate.py::score_content_quality",
232	            license="in-repo (POLARIS)", runnable="yes", role="baseline", family="polaris",
233	            loader=_load_polaris_heuristic,
234	            notes="incumbent floor, used as a ranker not a drop-gate",
235	        ),
236	        Candidate(
237	            name="dclm_fasttext",
238	            impl_id="mlfoundations/fasttext-oh-eli5",
239	            license="MIT (model) / Apache-2.0 (code)", runnable="yes", role="candidate",
240	            family="dclm", loader=_load_dclm_fasttext,
241	            notes="CPU fastText; REQUIRES numpy<2.0; P(__label__hq) is the weight",
242	            flags=["requires_numpy_lt_2"],
243	        ),
244	        Candidate(
245	            name="ultra_fineweb_fasttext",
246	            impl_id="openbmb/Ultra-FineWeb-classifier",
247	            license="Apache-2.0", runnable="yes", role="candidate", family="ultrafineweb",
248	            loader=_load_ultra_fineweb,
249	            notes="CPU fastText; REQUIRES numpy<2.0; Pred score (P high-quality) is the weight",
250	            flags=["requires_numpy_lt_2"],
251	        ),
252	        Candidate(
253	            name="fineweb_edu",
254	            impl_id="HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu-classifier",
255	            license="Apache-2.0", runnable="needs_gpu", role="candidate", family="finewebedu",
256	            loader=_load_fineweb_edu,
257	            notes="BERT/arctic-embed-m regression 0..5; educational PROXY, not clinical authority",
258	        ),
259	        Candidate(
260	            name="nemotron_cc_ensemble",
261	            impl_id="nvidia/nemocurator-fineweb-nemotron-4-edu-classifier + "
262	                    "nvidia/nemocurator-fineweb-mixtral-edu-classifier",
263	            license="NVIDIA Open Model License + Apache-2.0 (VERIFY commercial clause)",
264	            runnable="needs_gpu", role="candidate", family="nemotron",
265	            loader=_load_nemotron_cc,
266	            notes="mean-of-normalized 0..5 edu heads per Nemotron-CC paper",
267	            flags=["verify_commercial_license_before_adopt"],
268	        ),
269	        Candidate(
270	            name="essential_web_taxonomy",
271	            impl_id="EssentialAI/eai-taxonomy-0.5b",
272	            license="under-specified (card 401); base Qwen2.5-0.5B Apache-2.0 — VERIFY-BEFORE-ADOPT",
273	            runnable="needs_gpu", role="candidate", family="essentialweb",
274	            loader=_load_essential_web,
275	            notes="0.5B taxonomy SLM; reasoning-depth axis (NOT topic FDC field)",
276	            flags=["license_unverified_card_401", "parse_pending"],
277	        ),
278	        Candidate(
279	            name="glm52_llm_judge",
280	            impl_id="z-ai/glm-5.1 via OpenRouter (GLM-5.2 campaign backbone)",
281	            license="API (non-sovereign-as-judge; ceiling only)", runnable="no_key",
282	            role="yardstick_non_sovereign", family="glm", loader=_load_glm_judge,
283	            notes="ceiling yardstick; scored ONLY vs a different family's labels (no self-grade)",
284	        ),
285	    ]
286	
287	
288	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
289	# Negative controls (NOT candidates; exempt from semantic-direction liveness).
290	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
291	def constant_scorer(_text: str) -> float:
292	    return 0.5
293	
294	
295	def make_random_scorer(seed: int = 7) -> Callable[[str], float]:
296	    import random
297	    rng = random.Random(seed)
298	    return lambda _text: rng.random()
299	
300	
301	def _adjudicated_rows(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
302	    """Rows whose label has completed two-family + spot-check adjudication and so carry a
303	    scored_label. run_bakeoff scores ONLY these — a rubric proposal is never the scored label."""
304	    out = []
305	    for r in rows:
306	        if r.get("label_status") == "adjudicated" and r.get("scored_label") in (0, 1):
307	            out.append({**r, "label": r["scored_label"]})
308	    return out
309	
310	
311	def score_candidate(cand: Candidate, scoring_rows: list[dict]) -> dict:
312	    """Load + liveness-gate + score one candidate. Honest skip for no_key / needs_gpu-without-gpu."""
313	    result = {"name": cand.name, "role": cand.role, "runnable": cand.runnable,
314	              "impl_id": cand.impl_id, "license": cand.license, "family": cand.family,
315	              "flags": list(cand.flags), "status": None, "auc": None}
316	
317	    # No-self-grade guard (advisor pt 2): an LLM-judge candidate must not be a label-adjudication
318	    # family — else it grades its own labels. Assert structurally so a future judge swap can't
319	    # silently reintroduce circularity.
320	    if cand.role.startswith("yardstick") or cand.family == "glm":
321	        if cand.family in LABEL_ADJUDICATION_FAMILIES:
322	            raise gate0.GateZeroQualityError(
323	                f"NO-SELF-GRADE violation: judge candidate family {cand.family!r} is in the "
324	                f"label-adjudication families {set(LABEL_ADJUDICATION_FAMILIES)} — it would grade "
325	                f"its own labels. Use a different-family label set for this candidate."
326	            )
327	
328	    if cand.runnable == "no_key":
329	        result["status"] = "skipped_no_key"
330	        return result
331	
332	    if cand.loader is None:
333	        result["status"] = "skipped_no_loader"
334	        return result
335	
336	    if cand.runnable == "needs_gpu" and not _gpu_available():
337	        result["status"] = "skipped_needs_gpu"
338	        return result
339	
340	    # Load -> scorer. A load failure is fail-loud non-functional, never a fake score.
341	    try:
342	        scorer = cand.loader()
343	    except Exception as exc:
344	        result["status"] = "load_failed"
345	        result["error"] = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
346	        return result
347	
348	    # Per-candidate LIVENESS canary (GATE-0 half B). Raises fail-loud on stub/constant/wrong-dir.
349	    try:
350	        live = gate0.assert_candidate_live(
351	            cand.name, scorer, _AUTHORITATIVE_CANARY_DOC, _GARBAGE_CANARY_DOC
352	        )
353	        result["liveness"] = live
354	    except gate0.GateZeroQualityError as exc:
355	        result["status"] = "liveness_failed"
356	        result["error"] = str(exc)
357	        return result
358	
359	    if not scoring_rows:
360	        result["status"] = "no_adjudicated_rows"
361	        return result
362	
363	    scored = [{**r, "score": float(scorer(r["post_extraction_body"]))} for r in scoring_rows]
364	    metric = gate0.paired_within_cell_auc(scored, score_key="score")
365	    result["n_pairs"] = metric["n_pairs"]
366	    result["n_cells_scored"] = metric["n_cells_scored"]
367	    result["n_cells_skipped"] = metric["n_cells_skipped"]
368	    # Zero-scorable-cell guard: a within-(topic x source_type) cell scores only when it holds
369	    # BOTH an authoritative and a spam item. If no cell qualifies, paired_within_cell_auc returns
370	    # auc=None — emit status 'no_scorable_cells', NEVER 'scored' with a misleading number. This is
371	    # the same fail-loud spirit as the provisional path: a ranking over empty cells is meaningless
372	    # (and would re-admit the source-type proxy this metric exists to kill).
373	    if metric["auc"] is None or metric["n_pairs"] == 0:
374	        result["status"] = "no_scorable_cells"
375	        result["auc"] = None
376	        return result
377	    result["status"] = "scored"
378	    result["auc"] = metric["auc"]
379	    return result
380	
381	
382	def _gpu_available() -> bool:
383	    try:
384	        import torch
385	        return bool(torch.cuda.is_available())
386	    except Exception:
387	        return False
388	
389	
390	def run(*, fixture_path: str, out_path: str, registry: Optional[list[Candidate]] = None) -> dict:
391	    """End-to-end: GATE-0 math canary -> load fixture -> score adjudicated rows -> ranked JSON."""
392	    # GATE-0 half A (scorer-math) FIRST. Raises fail-loud if mis-wired.
393	    math_report = gate0.run_scorer_math_canary()
394	
395	    rows = build_fixture.load_fixture(fixture_path)
396	    scoring_rows = _adjudicated_rows(rows)
397	    provisional = len(scoring_rows) == 0
398	
399	    registry = registry or build_registry()
400	    candidate_results = [score_candidate(c, scoring_rows) for c in registry]
401	
402	    # Negative controls: assert they land near 0.5 — only meaningful when the fixture HAS scorable
403	    # within-cell pairs. If no cell holds both classes the control AUC is undefined (None); the
404	    # near-half assertion is undefined too, so we record controls as skipped rather than fail on a
405	    # degenerate fixture (the no_scorable_cells guard below carries that signal loudly).
406	    control_results = []
407	    if scoring_rows:
408	        for ctrl_name, ctrl in (("constant_0.5", constant_scorer),
409	                                ("random_seeded", make_random_scorer())):
410	            scored = [{**r, "score": float(ctrl(r["post_extraction_body"]))} for r in scoring_rows]
411	            metric = gate0.paired_within_cell_auc(scored, score_key="score")
412	            if metric["auc"] is None or metric["n_pairs"] == 0:
413	                control_results.append({"name": ctrl_name, "role": "control", "auc": None,
414	                                        "status": "skipped_no_scorable_cells"})
415	                continue
416	            gate0.assert_control_near_half(ctrl_name, metric["auc"])
417	            control_results.append({"name": ctrl_name, "role": "control", "auc": metric["auc"],
418	                                    "status": "scored_near_half"})
419	
420	    scored_ok = [r for r in candidate_results if r["status"] == "scored" and r["auc"] is not None]
421	    ranked = sorted(scored_ok, key=lambda r: r["auc"], reverse=True)
422	
423	    # Top-level fail-loud flag: if rows were adjudicated yet EVERY runnable candidate landed in
424	    # 'no_scorable_cells', the fixture has no within-(topic x source_type) cell with both classes
425	    # — the metric cannot rank anything. Surface it loudly rather than ship an empty ranking.
426	    runnable_attempts = [r for r in candidate_results
427	                         if r["status"] in ("scored", "no_scorable_cells")]
428	    all_no_cells = bool(runnable_attempts) and all(
429	        r["status"] == "no_scorable_cells" for r in runnable_attempts
430	    )
431	
432	    report = {
433	        "layer": "quality_weight",
434	        "gate0_scorer_math": math_report,
435	        "fixture": fixture_path,
436	        "n_fixture_rows": len(rows),
437	        "n_adjudicated_rows": len(scoring_rows),
438	        "provisional": provisional,
439	        "provisional_reason": (
440	            "0 adjudicated rows: fixture labels are 'proposed' (two-family + operator spot-check "
441	            "pending). AUCs not emitted; candidates show liveness/skip status only. Adjudicate "
442	            "labels then re-run for a final ranking."
443	        ) if provisional else None,
444	        "no_scorable_cells_for_all_candidates": all_no_cells,
445	        "no_scorable_cells_reason": (
446	            "All runnable candidates returned no_scorable_cells: no within-(topic x source_type) "
447	            "cell holds BOTH an authoritative and a spam item. Root cause: the proposed-label "
448	            "rubric labels quality by DOMAIN IDENTITY, which is collinear with source_type, so "
449	            "same-source-type quality contrasts are absent. The two-family adjudication must "
450	            "deliberately add same-source-type contrasts (legit vs predatory journal, real gov vs "
451	            "gov-mimic farm, reputable outlet vs SEO health farm) before a ranking is valid."
452	        ) if all_no_cells else None,
453	        # Secondary metric KNOWN-NOT-YET-IMPLEMENTED (brief §4): recall-preservation guard =
454	        # fraction of adjudicated-authoritative sources landing in the bottom weight-decile must
455	        # stay near zero (weight never demotes a real authoritative source). Moot until adjudicated
456	        # rows exist; flagged here so it is not silently dropped.
457	        "recall_preservation_guard": "not_yet_implemented (pending adjudicated rows; brief §4 "
458	                                      "secondary — authoritative-in-bottom-decile must be ~0)",
459	        "candidates": candidate_results,
460	        "controls": control_results,
461	        "ranking": [{"name": r["name"], "auc": r["auc"]} for r in ranked],
462	    }
463	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path) or ".", exist_ok=True)
464	    with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
465	        json.dump(report, fh, indent=2)
466	    return report
467	
468	
469	def main(argv=None) -> int:
470	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
471	    ap.add_argument("--fixture", default=os.path.join(_HERE, "clinical_quality_weight_fixture.jsonl"))
472	    ap.add_argument("--out", default=os.path.join(_HERE, "quality_weight_results.json"))
473	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
474	    try:
475	        report = run(fixture_path=args.fixture, out_path=args.out)
476	    except gate0.GateZeroQualityError as exc:
477	        print(f"GATE-0 FAIL (no score trusted): {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
478	        return 1
479	    except FileNotFoundError as exc:
480	        print(f"{exc}", file=sys.stderr)
481	        return 1
482	    print(f"quality_weight bake-off: {len(report['candidates'])} candidates, "
483	          f"{report['n_adjudicated_rows']} adjudicated rows "
484	          f"(provisional={report['provisional']})")
485	    for c in report["candidates"]:
486	        auc = "n/a" if c["auc"] is None else f"{c['auc']:.4f}"
487	        print(f"  [{c['status']:>18}] {c['name']:<24} auc={auc}")
488	    for ctrl in report["controls"]:
489	        cauc = "n/a" if ctrl["auc"] is None else f"{ctrl['auc']:.4f}"
490	        print(f"  [{ctrl['status']:>18}] {ctrl['name']:<24} auc={cauc}")
491	    print(f"  -> {args.out}")
492	    return 0
493	
494	
495	if __name__ == "__main__":
496	    raise SystemExit(main())
497

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""OFFLINE stub smoke test for the quality_weight bake-off (I-ret-002 #1294, §4).
3	
4	Proves, with NO GPU / NO network / NO model download (all model/API loaders MOCKED):
5	  1. all four layer files py_compile;
6	  2. the GATE-0 scorer-math canary PASSES (perfect=1.0 / inverted=0.0 / constant=0.5 /
7	     random-in-band / within-cell-kills-source-type-prior);
8	  3. the GATE-0 per-candidate LIVENESS canary correctly FAILS LOUD on a simulated STUB candidate
9	     (a constant-0.5 scorer) — the anti-drb_72 proof — AND PASSES on a synthetic real scorer;
10	  4. the negative controls (constant / random) land near 0.5;
11	  5. a mini end-to-end run on synthetic ADJUDICATED rows ranks a good scorer above a noisy one
12	     while a stub candidate is recorded as liveness_failed (never given a fake AUC);
13	  6. build_fixture's objective rubric labels real domains correctly (authoritative vs UGC/spam),
14	     and a 'proposed' fixture is NOT scored (provisional), all offline.
15	
16	Exit 0 on success; non-zero with a printed reason on any failure.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import os
22	import py_compile
23	import sys
24	import tempfile
25	import json
26	
27	_HERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
28	if _HERE not in sys.path:
29	    sys.path.insert(0, _HERE)
30	
31	import gate0  # noqa: E402
32	import build_fixture  # noqa: E402
33	import run_bakeoff  # noqa: E402
34	
35	_FAILURES: list[str] = []
36	
37	
38	def _check(name: str, ok: bool, detail: str = "") -> None:
39	    status = "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
40	    print(f"  [{status}] {name}" + (f" :: {detail}" if detail else ""))
41	    if not ok:
42	        _FAILURES.append(name)
43	
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# 1. py_compile all four files
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	def test_py_compile() -> None:
49	    print("[1] py_compile all four layer files")
50	    for fname in ("gate0.py", "build_fixture.py", "run_bakeoff.py", "smoke_test.py"):
51	        path = os.path.join(_HERE, fname)
52	        try:
53	            py_compile.compile(path, doraise=True)
54	            _check(f"py_compile {fname}", True)
55	        except py_compile.PyCompileError as exc:
56	            _check(f"py_compile {fname}", False, str(exc))
57	
58	
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	# 2. scorer-math canary PASSES
61	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	def test_scorer_math_canary() -> None:
63	    print("[2] GATE-0 scorer-math canary")
64	    try:
65	        report = gate0.run_scorer_math_canary()
66	        names = {c["name"]: c["ok"] for c in report["checks"]}
67	        _check("scorer_math all checks pass", all(names.values()), str(names))
68	    except gate0.GateZeroQualityError as exc:
69	        _check("scorer_math all checks pass", False, str(exc))
70	
71	
72	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
73	# 3. liveness canary FAILS on a stub, PASSES on a real scorer  (anti-drb_72)
74	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	def _good_synthetic_scorer(text: str) -> float:
76	    """A synthetic REAL classifier: rewards clinical/regulatory signal, penalizes spam markers.
77	    Used to prove the liveness canary lets a genuine varying classifier through."""
78	    t = text.lower()
79	    good = sum(t.count(k) for k in ("trial", "confidence interval", "endpoint", "dose",
80	                                    "contraindication", "hba1c", "placebo", "p<0.001"))
81	    bad = sum(t.count(k) for k in ("cookie", "subscribe", "buy now", "discount", "click here",
82	                                   "best deals", "sign up"))
83	    # bounded, varying, monotone in (good-bad)
84	    return 0.5 + 0.05 * (good - bad)
85	
86	
87	def _constant_stub_scorer(_text: str) -> float:
88	    """A SILENT-STUB candidate: returns a constant for every doc. This is the drb_72 failure mode
89	    — it would still produce a real-looking AUC. The liveness canary MUST reject it."""
90	    return 0.5
91	
92	
93	def test_liveness_canary() -> None:
94	    print("[3] GATE-0 per-candidate liveness canary (anti-drb_72)")
95	    auth = run_bakeoff._AUTHORITATIVE_CANARY_DOC
96	    junk = run_bakeoff._GARBAGE_CANARY_DOC
97	
98	    # 3a: a real synthetic scorer PASSES liveness
99	    try:
100	        live = gate0.assert_candidate_live("synthetic_good", _good_synthetic_scorer, auth, junk)
101	        _check("real scorer passes liveness", live["ok"] and live["gap"] > 0,
102	               f"gap={live['gap']:.3f}")
103	    except gate0.GateZeroQualityError as exc:
104	        _check("real scorer passes liveness", False, str(exc))
105	
106	    # 3b: a constant STUB candidate MUST FAIL LOUD (raise) — this is the load-bearing check
107	    raised = False
108	    try:
109	        gate0.assert_candidate_live("constant_stub", _constant_stub_scorer, auth, junk)
110	    except gate0.GateZeroQualityError as exc:
111	        raised = True
112	        detail = str(exc)[:90]
113	    _check("constant STUB candidate FAILS LOUD", raised,
114	           detail if raised else "stub was NOT rejected — canary too weak (the drb_72 hole)")
115	
116	    # 3c: a load-fail candidate MUST FAIL LOUD (propagate, never a low score)
117	    def _exploding_scorer(_text: str) -> float:
118	        raise RuntimeError("simulated model load/runtime failure")
119	
120	    raised2 = False
121	    try:
122	        gate0.assert_candidate_live("load_fail", _exploding_scorer, auth, junk)
123	    except gate0.GateZeroQualityError:
124	        raised2 = True
125	    _check("load-fail candidate FAILS LOUD", raised2,
126	           "raised" if raised2 else "load failure produced a score instead of raising")
127	
128	    # 3d: a WRONG-DIRECTION scorer (ranks garbage above authoritative) MUST FAIL LOUD
129	    raised3 = False
130	    try:
131	        gate0.assert_candidate_live("inverted", lambda t: -_good_synthetic_scorer(t), auth, junk)
132	    except gate0.GateZeroQualityError:
133	        raised3 = True
134	    _check("wrong-direction candidate FAILS LOUD", raised3,
135	           "raised" if raised3 else "garbage-over-authoritative was accepted")
136	
137	
138	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
139	# 4. negative controls land near 0.5
140	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
141	def test_controls_near_half() -> None:
142	    print("[4] negative controls near 0.5")
143	    # map scored_label -> label exactly as run_bakeoff does for adjudicated rows
144	    rows = run_bakeoff._adjudicated_rows(_synthetic_adjudicated_rows())
145	    for name, scorer in (("constant_0.5", run_bakeoff.constant_scorer),
146	                         ("random_seeded", run_bakeoff.make_random_scorer())):
147	        scored = [{**r, "score": scorer(r["post_extraction_body"])} for r in rows]
148	        auc = gate0.paired_within_cell_auc(scored, score_key="score")["auc"]
149	        try:
150	            gate0.assert_control_near_half(name, auc)
151	            _check(f"control {name} near 0.5", True, f"auc={auc:.3f}")
152	        except gate0.GateZeroQualityError as exc:
153	            _check(f"control {name} near 0.5", False, str(exc))
154	
155	
156	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
157	# 5. mini end-to-end with MOCKED loaders on synthetic ADJUDICATED rows
158	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
159	def _synthetic_adjudicated_rows() -> list[dict]:
160	    """Synthetic fixture: within each (topic x source_type) cell, an authoritative body (clinical
161	    signal) and a spam body (marketing junk). label_status=adjudicated so run_bakeoff scores them.
162	    NO real snapshot, NO model — pure offline."""
163	    auth_body = run_bakeoff._AUTHORITATIVE_CANARY_DOC
164	    spam_body = run_bakeoff._GARBAGE_CANARY_DOC
165	    rows = []
166	    for topic in ("topicA", "topicB", "topicC"):
167	        for st in ("peer_reviewed_publisher", "gov_regulator"):
168	            rows.append({"source_id": f"{topic}:{st}:auth", "topic_id": topic, "source_type": st,
169	                         "post_extraction_body": auth_body + f" study {topic}",
170	                         "label_status": "adjudicated", "scored_label": 1})
171	            rows.append({"source_id": f"{topic}:{st}:spam", "topic_id": topic, "source_type": st,
172	                         "post_extraction_body": spam_body + f" {topic}",
173	                         "label_status": "adjudicated", "scored_label": 0})
174	    return rows
175	
176	
177	def test_end_to_end_mocked() -> None:
178	    print("[5] mini end-to-end with MOCKED loaders (no GPU/net/download)")
179	    rows = _synthetic_adjudicated_rows()
180	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
181	        fixture_path = os.path.join(td, "fixture.jsonl")
182	        with open(fixture_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
183	            for r in rows:
184	                fh.write(json.dumps(r) + "\n")
185	        out_path = os.path.join(td, "results.json")
186	
187	        # Build a MOCKED registry: loaders return synthetic scorers (no torch/fasttext/API).
188	        registry = [
189	            run_bakeoff.Candidate(
190	                name="good_model", impl_id="mock://good", license="mock", runnable="yes",
191	                role="candidate", family="mockfamily",
192	                loader=lambda: _good_synthetic_scorer),
193	            run_bakeoff.Candidate(
194	                name="noisy_model", impl_id="mock://noisy", license="mock", runnable="yes",
195	                role="candidate", family="mockfamily2",
196	                loader=lambda: (lambda t: _good_synthetic_scorer(t) * 0.2
197	                                + run_bakeoff.make_random_scorer(3)(t) * 0.8)),
198	            run_bakeoff.Candidate(
199	                name="stub_model", impl_id="mock://stub", license="mock", runnable="yes",
200	                role="candidate", family="mockfamily3",
201	                loader=lambda: _constant_stub_scorer),  # MUST be liveness_failed, never scored
202	            run_bakeoff.Candidate(
203	                name="nokey_judge", impl_id="mock://judge", license="api", runnable="no_key",
204	                role="yardstick_non_sovereign", family="glm",
205	                loader=lambda: (_ for _ in ()).throw(RuntimeError("no key"))),  # skipped_no_key
206	        ]
207	        report = run_bakeoff.run(fixture_path=fixture_path, out_path=out_path, registry=registry)
208	
209	        by_name = {c["name"]: c for c in report["candidates"]}
210	        _check("good_model scored", by_name["good_model"]["status"] == "scored",

1	"""Resolve the ``gate0_lineage`` seam robustly.
2	
3	The reranker layer REUSES ``scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py`` (the I-qgen-001 lineage/idx
4	binding seam — brief §6 GATE-0). In the merged tree it is a sibling package module and imports
5	plainly. In an isolated worktree cut BEFORE that file landed on main, the working copy may not
6	have it materialized yet (a transient environment artifact, not a code defect). This helper makes
7	the import resilient WITHOUT hardcoding any absolute path:
8	
9	  1. Try the normal package import ``scripts.dr_benchmark.gate0_lineage``.
10	  2. If absent, locate the file on disk by walking up from this module to a ``scripts/dr_benchmark``
11	     dir, then by asking git for the repo's OTHER linked worktree paths (the main checkout shares
12	     the same object store and DOES carry the seam). Load it by file path via importlib.
13	  3. If it is genuinely nowhere, raise ImportError (fail loud) — never a silent stub.
14	
15	The returned module is the real ``gate0_lineage`` with SLUG_TO_IDX, assert_drb_slug_registered,
16	is_benchmark_slug, GateZeroLineageError.
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import importlib
22	import importlib.util
23	import os
24	import subprocess
25	import sys
26	from types import ModuleType
27	
28	
29	_RELATIVE = os.path.join("scripts", "dr_benchmark", "gate0_lineage.py")
30	
31	
32	def _load_from_file(path: str) -> ModuleType:
33	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("gate0_lineage", path)
34	    if spec is None or spec.loader is None:
35	        raise ImportError(f"could not build import spec for {path}")
36	    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
37	    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
38	    return mod
39	
40	
41	def _candidate_paths() -> list[str]:
42	    paths: list[str] = []
43	    here = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
44	    # Walk up looking for a co-located scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py.
45	    cur = here
46	    for _ in range(8):
47	        cand = os.path.join(cur, _RELATIVE)
48	        if cand not in paths:
49	            paths.append(cand)
50	        parent = os.path.dirname(cur)
51	        if parent == cur:
52	            break
53	        cur = parent
54	    # Ask git for every linked worktree root (the main checkout carries the seam even when this
55	    # isolated worktree was cut before it landed). Pure read; never writes.
56	    try:
57	        out = subprocess.run(
58	            ["git", "worktree", "list", "--porcelain"],
59	            cwd=here, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=20, check=False,
60	        )
61	        for line in out.stdout.splitlines():
62	            if line.startswith("worktree "):
63	                root = line[len("worktree "):].strip()
64	                cand = os.path.join(root, _RELATIVE)
65	                if cand not in paths:
66	                    paths.append(cand)
67	    except Exception:
68	        pass
69	    return paths
70	
71	
72	def load_gate0_lineage() -> ModuleType:
73	    """Return the real gate0_lineage module, however the tree is laid out. Fail loud if absent."""
74	    try:
75	        return importlib.import_module("scripts.dr_benchmark.gate0_lineage")
76	    except Exception:
77	        pass
78	    for path in _candidate_paths():
79	        if os.path.isfile(path):
80	            return _load_from_file(path)
81	    raise ImportError(
82	        "gate0_lineage seam not found (scripts/dr_benchmark/gate0_lineage.py). It is the "
83	        "I-qgen-001 lineage binding the reranker layer REUSES; it must be present in the merged "
84	        "tree. Searched package import + every linked git worktree root."
85	    )
86	
87	
88	# Resolve once at import; re-export the public names so callers do a normal ``from ... import X``.
89	_mod = load_gate0_lineage()
90	SLUG_TO_IDX = _mod.SLUG_TO_IDX
91	GateZeroLineageError = _mod.GateZeroLineageError
92	assert_drb_slug_registered = _mod.assert_drb_slug_registered
93	is_benchmark_slug = _mod.is_benchmark_slug
94

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-ret-002 (#1294) reranker-layer ISOLATION bake-off — labelled ground-truth fixture builder.
3	
4	This module builds (or loads) the FROZEN pre-rerank candidate-pool fixture that every reranker
5	candidate is scored on. Per the brief (§6 "reranker") the fixture carries, per gold idx:
6	
7	  * a byte-identical, banked pre-rerank candidate pool (so every reranker sees the EXACT same
8	    rows; no live re-retrieval per candidate — that would leak query-gen variance and break
9	    isolation), AND
10	  * per-candidate (claim-level RELEVANCE x INDEPENDENT CREDIBILITY) gain labels in {0,1,2,3}.
11	
12	Two hard correctness rules from the brief + the Codex iter-2 resolution, encoded structurally
13	here so they cannot drift:
14	
15	  1. CREDIBILITY LABEL INDEPENDENCE. The credibility label is produced by an INDEPENDENT
16	     two-family adjudication over the pre-rerank pool — it is NEVER read from POLARIS's own
17	     ``tier`` / ``authority_score`` metadata (grading a reranker's credibility ordering against
18	     POLARIS tier would just reward agreement with POLARIS). Independence is enforced primarily by
19	     PROCESS: this module NEVER computes a credibility label from tier — the SCORED label is the
20	     two-family-adjudicated + operator-spot-checked value LOADED from the separate annotation file,
21	     and the judge only PROPOSES. Two BACKSTOPS catch a lazily tier-derived annotation:
22	     ``_assert_no_polaris_tier_leak`` (per-row literal-echo check) and
23	     ``assert_credibility_not_tier_derived`` (aggregate: fail loud if the whole annotation is a
24	     deterministic function of the T1..T7 tier band). They are backstops, not the primary guarantee.
25	
26	  2. CLAIM-LEVEL RELEVANCE RESOLUTION (the idx66 collapse fix). DRB-II ``info_recall`` findings
27	     without a quoted title (idx66: only 3/48 carry a title) are judge-mapped to their supporting
28	     source at the CLAIM level, never title-matched and silently dropped. Per-idx gold-N is
29	     reported so a thin denominator is visible (never a hidden drop).
30	
31	DEMOTE-ONLY / WEIGHT-NOT-FILTER (§-1.3). The raw-json "off-topic-drop threshold" facet is
32	SUPERSEDED and SCRUBBED: off-topic items get gain 0 (they sink in graded NDCG), they are NEVER
33	removed. There is no drop threshold anywhere in this fixture. ``assert_demote_only_invariant``
34	fail-loud-checks that the gain map contains a 0 (demotion) and no "drop" key ever appears.
35	
36	HONEST SCOPE. Real two-family credibility annotation is a separate parallel judge job (the brief
37	budgets it as such). This builder constructs the frozen pool + the adjudication SCHEMA/HOOKS +
38	the annotation LOADER. If the annotation file is absent it FAILS LOUD (never fabricates labels);
39	the offline smoke supplies a tiny synthetic annotation so it can prove the math + schema without
40	the real judge job. That honest gap is reported, never hidden.
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import argparse
46	import hashlib
47	import json
48	import os
49	import sys
50	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
51	from typing import Any, Optional
52	
53	# --- reuse the lineage seam (idx binding; fail-loud on an unregistered benchmark slug) --------
54	_THIS_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
55	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(_THIS_DIR, "..", "..", "..", ".."))
56	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:
57	    sys.path.insert(0, _REPO_ROOT)
58	
59	from scripts.dr_benchmark.retrieval_bakeoff.reranker._lineage_seam import (  # noqa: E402
60	    SLUG_TO_IDX,
61	    GateZeroLineageError,
62	    assert_drb_slug_registered,
63	    is_benchmark_slug,
64	)
65	
66	# The four gold-bearing benchmark slugs the reranker bake-off binds (brief §6 + raw-json
67	# holding_condition: drb_72->56, drb_75->62, drb_76->66, drb_78->72). Resolved from the lineage
68	# seam, never hardcoded as an idx here.
69	GOLD_SLUGS: tuple[str, ...] = (
70	    "drb_72_ai_labor",
71	    "drb_75_metal_ions_cvd",
72	    "drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc",
73	    "drb_78_parkinsons_dbs",
74	)
75	
76	# Pre-registered graded-gain scale. RELEVANCE (claim-level info_recall support) x INDEPENDENT
77	# CREDIBILITY combine DETERMINISTICALLY into a single gain in {0,1,2,3}. Pre-registered here so it
78	# is NOT reverse-engineered to favour a candidate (advisor #11). off_topic -> 0 ALWAYS (demotion,
79	# never a drop). The mapping is intentionally explicit and total over the label cross-product.
80	#
81	#   relevance label : off_topic | on_topic_irrelevant | supporting | strongly_supporting
82	#   credibility label: spam | low | medium | high   (INDEPENDENT of POLARIS tier)
83	#
84	# gain = 0 unless the source actually SUPPORTS a gold claim; among supporting sources, gain rises
85	# with credibility. on_topic-but-non-supporting (e.g. on-topic SEO spam) stays low. This rewards
86	# "credible AND relevant ranked first", with off-topic demoted to the floor — never dropped.
87	RELEVANCE_LABELS: tuple[str, ...] = (
88	    "off_topic",
89	    "on_topic_irrelevant",
90	    "supporting",
91	    "strongly_supporting",
92	)
93	CREDIBILITY_LABELS: tuple[str, ...] = ("spam", "low", "medium", "high")
94	
95	GRADED_GAIN_TABLE: dict[tuple[str, str], int] = {
96	    # off_topic -> 0 for ANY credibility (demote-only; off-topic never removed, never rewarded).
97	    ("off_topic", "spam"): 0,
98	    ("off_topic", "low"): 0,
99	    ("off_topic", "medium"): 0,
100	    ("off_topic", "high"): 0,
101	    # on-topic but NOT supporting a gold claim (on-topic spam / fluff): small or zero gain.
102	    ("on_topic_irrelevant", "spam"): 0,
103	    ("on_topic_irrelevant", "low"): 0,
104	    ("on_topic_irrelevant", "medium"): 1,
105	    ("on_topic_irrelevant", "high"): 1,
106	    # supporting a gold claim: gain scales with INDEPENDENT credibility.
107	    ("supporting", "spam"): 1,
108	    ("supporting", "low"): 1,
109	    ("supporting", "medium"): 2,
110	    ("supporting", "high"): 2,
111	    # strongly supporting a gold claim from a credible source: top gain.
112	    ("strongly_supporting", "spam"): 1,
113	    ("strongly_supporting", "low"): 2,
114	    ("strongly_supporting", "medium"): 2,
115	    ("strongly_supporting", "high"): 3,
116	}
117	
118	# A source labelled "required" (its gold claim has NO other supporting source in the pool) is the
119	# cardinal recall@K guard target: a reranker that pushes a required source out of the top-K starves
120	# the downstream basket. This mirrors the dedup 0.97 precision floor — a hard NON-REGRESSION gate.
121	REQUIRED_RECALL_FLOOR_RELATIVE_TO_BASELINE: float = 1.0  # must be >= baseline recall@K (no regression)
122	
123	
124	class FixtureError(RuntimeError):
125	    """Fail-loud error building/loading the reranker fixture (never a silent empty fixture)."""
126	
127	
128	@dataclass
129	class PoolCandidate:
130	    """One row in the FROZEN pre-rerank pool for a gold idx."""
131	
132	    cand_id: str
133	    title: str
134	    body: str  # extracted main content (held-fixed Trafilatura output, per holding_condition)
135	    url: str
136	    # SCORED labels — set ONLY by two-family adjudication (judge proposes, never sets). These are
137	    # loaded from the annotation file, NOT computed from POLARIS metadata.
138	    relevance_label: str = ""
139	    credibility_label: str = ""
140	    supports_claim_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)  # claim-level resolution
141	    required: bool = False  # sole supporter of its gold claim -> recall@K guard target
142	
143	    def gain(self) -> int:
144	        """DETERMINISTIC graded gain from the pre-registered table. Fail loud on an unknown label
145	        (never default to a believable middle value — that would mask a mislabel)."""
146	        key = (self.relevance_label, self.credibility_label)
147	        if key not in GRADED_GAIN_TABLE:
148	            raise FixtureError(
149	                f"cand {self.cand_id!r}: unknown (relevance,credibility) label pair {key!r}; "
150	                f"valid relevance={RELEVANCE_LABELS} credibility={CREDIBILITY_LABELS}. "
151	                f"The label must come from two-family adjudication, never a silent default."
152	            )
153	        return GRADED_GAIN_TABLE[key]
154	
155	
156	@dataclass
157	class GoldIdxFixture:
158	    """The frozen pool + labels for ONE gold idx."""
159	
160	    slug: str
161	    idx: int
162	    question: str
163	    pool: list[PoolCandidate]
164	    gold_claim_ids: list[str]  # claim-level info_recall ids (the denominator we report per idx)
165	
166	    def pool_sha(self) -> str:
167	        """Stable hash of the frozen pool ORDER+content (proves every candidate saw the same input)."""
168	        h = hashlib.sha256()
169	        for c in self.pool:
170	            h.update(c.cand_id.encode("utf-8"))
171	            h.update(b"\x00")
172	            h.update(c.body.encode("utf-8"))
173	            h.update(b"\x01")
174	        return h.hexdigest()
175	
176	    def gains(self) -> list[int]:
177	        return [c.gain() for c in self.pool]
178	
179	    def required_ids(self) -> list[str]:
180	        return [c.cand_id for c in self.pool if c.required]
181	
182	
183	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	# Invariant assertions (structural §-1.3 guards) — called by the builder AND by gate0/smoke.
185	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
186	def assert_demote_only_invariant(gain_table: dict[tuple[str, str], int]) -> None:
187	    """Fail loud unless the gain table is DEMOTE-ONLY (off_topic -> 0 for every credibility) and
188	    carries NO drop semantics. This is the structural scrub of the SUPERSEDED off-topic-drop facet
189	    (brief §6 BUILD-HANDOFF NOTE). A reranker may demote (gain 0), NEVER remove."""
190	    for cred in CREDIBILITY_LABELS:
191	        key = ("off_topic", cred)
192	        if gain_table.get(key) != 0:
193	            raise FixtureError(
194	                f"DEMOTE-ONLY violation: off_topic x {cred} must map to gain 0 (demotion), "
195	                f"got {gain_table.get(key)!r}. Off-topic items sink to the floor, never dropped."
196	            )
197	    # No key may encode a "drop"/"remove"/"filter" action — gains are integers only.
198	    for k, v in gain_table.items():
199	        if not isinstance(v, int) or v < 0:
200	            raise FixtureError(
201	                f"gain for {k!r} = {v!r} is not a non-negative int; a hard-drop / negative-gain "
202	                f"sentinel is a §-1.3 weight-not-filter breach and is forbidden."
203	            )
204	
205	
206	# POLARIS tier values are T1..T7. To catch a credibility label that was DERIVED from the tier (the
207	# obvious circular shortcut: T1/T2 -> "high", T3/T4 -> "medium", ...), we map tier -> the credibility
208	# band it would collapse to and fail loud if the scored label exactly matches that derivation for
209	# EVERY row. A single coincidental match is not proof of a leak (a genuinely-high source IS often
210	# T1); independence is enforced primarily by PROCESS (the scored label is loaded from the separate
211	# two-family annotation file, never computed from tier here). This guard is a BACKSTOP that catches
212	# the gross case where the annotation was lazily back-filled from tier.
213	_TIER_TO_CREDIBILITY_BAND: dict[str, str] = {
214	    "t1": "high", "t2": "high",
215	    "t3": "medium", "t4": "medium",
216	    "t5": "low", "t6": "low",
217	    "t7": "spam",
218	}
219	
220	
221	def _tier_derived_band(raw_row: dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]:
222	    """The credibility band a row's POLARIS tier WOULD collapse to (or None if no usable tier)."""
223	    for f in ("tier", "authority_tier", "credibility_tier"):
224	        v = str(raw_row.get(f) or "").strip().lower()
225	        if v in _TIER_TO_CREDIBILITY_BAND:
226	            return _TIER_TO_CREDIBILITY_BAND[v]
227	    return None
228	
229	
230	def _assert_no_polaris_tier_leak(raw_row: dict[str, Any], scored_credibility: str) -> None:
231	    """Per-row BACKSTOP against a credibility label trivially copied/derived from POLARIS tier
232	    metadata (the circular trap §6 forbids). Independence is PROCESS-enforced (scored labels come
233	    from the separate two-family annotation file). This catches the gross derivation: the scored
234	    band equals the tier-derived band AND a raw band string was literally echoed.
235	
236	    Note: a single match is NOT a leak (a T1 source legitimately IS 'high'). The aggregate guard
237	    ``assert_credibility_not_tier_derived`` (called over the whole pool) is the real independence
238	    check; this per-row helper only flags a literal field echo for fast feedback."""
239	    raw_echo_fields = ("authority_score", "credibility_label_from_tier")
240	    for f in raw_echo_fields:
241	        if f in raw_row and str(raw_row.get(f)).strip().lower() == str(scored_credibility).strip().lower():
242	            raise FixtureError(
243	                f"CREDIBILITY-INDEPENDENCE violation: scored credibility {scored_credibility!r} "
244	                f"literally echoes POLARIS metadata field {f!r}={raw_row.get(f)!r}. The credibility "
245	                f"label MUST be an independent two-family annotation, not POLARIS tier (brief §6)."
246	            )
247	
248	
249	def assert_credibility_not_tier_derived(
250	    rows_and_labels: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], str]],
251	) -> None:
252	    """AGGREGATE independence guard: fail loud if EVERY row whose POLARIS tier is known has a scored
253	    credibility label that exactly equals the tier-derived band — i.e. the whole annotation is a
254	    deterministic function of tier (the circular shortcut). Requires at least one tier-bearing row;
255	    a partial match is allowed (a credible T1 source genuinely mapping to 'high' is fine)."""
256	    derivable = [
257	        (band, scored)
258	        for raw, scored in rows_and_labels
259	        if (band := _tier_derived_band(raw)) is not None
260	    ]
261	    if len(derivable) < 2:
262	        return  # too few tier-bearing rows to conclude a systematic derivation
263	    if all(band == scored for band, scored in derivable):
264	        raise FixtureError(
265	            "CREDIBILITY-INDEPENDENCE violation (aggregate): every tier-bearing row's scored "
266	            "credibility label exactly equals its POLARIS tier-derived band — the annotation is a "
267	            "deterministic function of POLARIS tier, not an independent two-family judgment (§6). "
268	            f"({len(derivable)} rows checked.)"
269	        )
270	
271	
272	def _resolve_idx(slug: str) -> int:
273	    """idx via the lineage seam; fail loud on an unregistered benchmark slug (drb_72 anti-pattern)."""
274	    if is_benchmark_slug(slug):
275	        assert_drb_slug_registered(slug)
276	    if slug not in SLUG_TO_IDX:
277	        raise FixtureError(
278	            f"slug {slug!r} has no canonical idx in SLUG_TO_IDX; resolve it from the gold file "
279	            f"before adding it (never guess the idx)."
280	        )
281	    return SLUG_TO_IDX[slug]
282	
283	
284	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	# Annotation loader — the SCORED labels (two-family adjudicated, judge proposes only).
286	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
287	def _load_pool_for_idx(slug: str, pool_dir: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
288	    """Load the FROZEN pre-rerank pool rows for a slug from ``<pool_dir>/<slug>.pool.jsonl``.
289	
290	    Each row = {cand_id, title, body, url, ...optional POLARIS metadata...}. Fail loud if missing
291	    or empty (never an empty pool silently)."""
292	    path = os.path.join(pool_dir, f"{slug}.pool.jsonl")
293	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
294	        raise FixtureError(
295	            f"frozen pool not found for {slug!r}: {path}. Build it ONCE from the banked "
296	            f"corpus_snapshot (held-fixed IterResearch queries) before running the bake-off."
297	        )
298	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
299	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
300	        for line in fh:
301	            line = line.strip()
302	            if line:
303	                rows.append(json.loads(line))
304	    if not rows:
305	        raise FixtureError(f"frozen pool for {slug!r} is EMPTY ({path}); refusing to score an empty pool.")
306	    return rows
307	
308	
309	def _load_annotation_for_idx(slug: str, annot_dir: str) -> dict[str, dict[str, Any]]:
310	    """Load the two-family-adjudicated SCORED labels for a slug from
311	    ``<annot_dir>/<slug>.labels.jsonl``: cand_id -> {relevance_label, credibility_label,
312	    supports_claim_ids, required, adjudication{...}}. Judge PROPOSES only; this file holds the
313	    adjudicated value. Fail loud if missing (never fabricate labels)."""
314	    path = os.path.join(annot_dir, f"{slug}.labels.jsonl")
315	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
316	        raise FixtureError(
317	            f"two-family credibility/relevance annotation not found for {slug!r}: {path}. This is "
318	            f"the separate parallel judge job (brief execution plan); it must be produced + "
319	            f"operator-spot-checked before the bake-off scores trust this idx. NOT fabricated."
320	        )
321	    out: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
322	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
323	        for line in fh:
324	            line = line.strip()
325	            if not line:
326	                continue
327	            rec = json.loads(line)
328	            cid = rec.get("cand_id")
329	            if not cid:
330	                raise FixtureError(f"annotation row missing cand_id in {path}: {rec!r}")
331	            out[cid] = rec
332	    return out
333	
334	
335	def _load_gold_claims_for_idx(idx: int, info_recall_dir: str) -> list[str]:
336	    """Load the claim-level info_recall gold-claim ids for an idx from
337	    ``<info_recall_dir>/idx_<idx>.claims.json`` (a list of claim ids). Claim-level resolution fixes
338	    the idx66 collapse (untitled findings judge-mapped to their source). Fail loud if missing."""
339	    path = os.path.join(info_recall_dir, f"idx_{idx}.claims.json")
340	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
341	        raise FixtureError(
342	            f"info_recall gold claims not found for idx={idx}: {path}. Build claim-level gold "
343	            f"(judge-map untitled findings to supporting source; report per-idx gold-N)."
344	        )
345	    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as fh:
346	        claims = json.load(fh)
347	    if not isinstance(claims, list) or not claims:
348	        raise FixtureError(f"info_recall gold claims for idx={idx} must be a non-empty list ({path}).")
349	    return [str(c) for c in claims]
350	
351	
352	def build_idx_fixture(
353	    slug: str,
354	    *,
355	    pool_dir: str,
356	    annot_dir: str,
357	    info_recall_dir: str,
358	) -> GoldIdxFixture:
359	    """Build the frozen-pool + SCORED-label fixture for ONE gold idx. The judge only PROPOSES;
360	    the scored labels come from the two-family annotation file. Credibility independence + claim-
361	    level resolution + demote-only are enforced structurally."""
362	    idx = _resolve_idx(slug)
363	    pool_rows = _load_pool_for_idx(slug, pool_dir)
364	    annot = _load_annotation_for_idx(slug, annot_dir)
365	    gold_claim_ids = _load_gold_claims_for_idx(idx, info_recall_dir)
366	
367	    question = ""
368	    pool: list[PoolCandidate] = []
369	    rows_and_creds: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], str]] = []
370	    for row in pool_rows:
371	        cid = row.get("cand_id")
372	        if not cid:
373	            raise FixtureError(f"pool row for {slug!r} missing cand_id: {row!r}")
374	        question = question or str(row.get("question") or "")
375	        a = annot.get(cid)
376	        if a is None:
377	            raise FixtureError(
378	                f"pool cand {cid!r} ({slug}) has NO adjudicated label; every scored row must be "
379	                f"two-family-confirmed (no silent default). Annotate it or exclude it explicitly."
380	            )
381	        rel = str(a.get("relevance_label") or "")
382	        cred = str(a.get("credibility_label") or "")
383	        if rel not in RELEVANCE_LABELS:
384	            raise FixtureError(f"cand {cid!r}: relevance_label {rel!r} not in {RELEVANCE_LABELS}")
385	        if cred not in CREDIBILITY_LABELS:
386	            raise FixtureError(f"cand {cid!r}: credibility_label {cred!r} not in {CREDIBILITY_LABELS}")
387	        # Independence backstop: scored credibility must not literally echo POLARIS tier metadata.
388	        _assert_no_polaris_tier_leak(row, cred)
389	        rows_and_creds.append((row, cred))
390	        pool.append(
391	            PoolCandidate(
392	                cand_id=str(cid),
393	                title=str(row.get("title") or ""),
394	                body=str(row.get("body") or ""),
395	                url=str(row.get("url") or ""),
396	                relevance_label=rel,
397	                credibility_label=cred,
398	                supports_claim_ids=[str(x) for x in (a.get("supports_claim_ids") or [])],
399	                required=bool(a.get("required", False)),
400	            )
401	        )
402	
403	    assert_demote_only_invariant(GRADED_GAIN_TABLE)
404	    # Aggregate independence guard: the annotation must NOT be a deterministic function of POLARIS
405	    # tier (the real circularity check; the per-row helper above only catches a literal echo).
406	    assert_credibility_not_tier_derived(rows_and_creds)
407	    fixture = GoldIdxFixture(
408	        slug=slug, idx=idx, question=question, pool=pool, gold_claim_ids=gold_claim_ids
409	    )
410	    # Touch gains so a mislabel fails loud at BUILD time, not silently at score time.
411	    _ = fixture.gains()
412	    return fixture
413	
414	
415	def build_all(
416	    *,
417	    slugs: tuple[str, ...] = GOLD_SLUGS,
418	    pool_dir: str,
419	    annot_dir: str,
420	    info_recall_dir: str,
421	) -> dict[str, GoldIdxFixture]:
422	    """Build the fixture for every gold idx. Reports per-idx gold-N (visible thin denominator)."""
423	    out: dict[str, GoldIdxFixture] = {}
424	    for slug in slugs:
425	        fx = build_idx_fixture(
426	            slug, pool_dir=pool_dir, annot_dir=annot_dir, info_recall_dir=info_recall_dir
427	        )
428	        out[slug] = fx
429	        print(
430	            f"[{slug}] idx={fx.idx} pool={len(fx.pool)} gold_claims(N)={len(fx.gold_claim_ids)} "
431	            f"required={len(fx.required_ids())} pool_sha={fx.pool_sha()[:16]}",
432	            flush=True,
433	        )
434	    return out
435	
436	
437	def main() -> int:
438	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Build the reranker ISOLATION bake-off fixture.")
439	    ap.add_argument("--pool-dir", required=True, help="dir of <slug>.pool.jsonl frozen pre-rerank pools")
440	    ap.add_argument("--annot-dir", required=True, help="dir of <slug>.labels.jsonl two-family labels")
441	    ap.add_argument("--info-recall-dir", required=True, help="dir of idx_<idx>.claims.json gold claims")
442	    ap.add_argument("--slugs", default=",".join(GOLD_SLUGS))
443	    args = ap.parse_args()
444	
445	    slugs = tuple(s for s in args.slugs.split(",") if s)
446	    try:
447	        build_all(
448	            slugs=slugs,
449	            pool_dir=args.pool_dir,
450	            annot_dir=args.annot_dir,
451	            info_recall_dir=args.info_recall_dir,
452	        )
453	    except (FixtureError, GateZeroLineageError) as exc:
454	        print(f"FIXTURE BUILD FAILED (fail-loud): {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
455	        return 2
456	    return 0
457	
458	
459	if __name__ == "__main__":
460	    raise SystemExit(main())
461

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-ret-002 (#1294) reranker layer — GATE-0 validity harness (the anti-drb_72 gate).
3	
4	Nothing in the bake-off is trusted until BOTH canaries below are green:
5	
6	  A. SCORER-MATH canary (credibility-graded NDCG@K). Known ordering -> known score:
7	       * ideal ordering              -> NDCG == 1.0   (normalization)
8	       * a hand-built fractional case -> NDCG == a HAND-COMPUTED value (the real discriminator;
9	         a scorer that always returns 1.0 passes the ideal case but FAILS this one).
10	     Plus the required-source recall@K guard math (a known eviction -> known recall).
11	
12	  B. PER-CANDIDATE MODEL-WIRING LIVENESS canary. Each LOADED reranker is handed one obviously
13	     relevant doc and one obvious-junk doc for a fixed query and MUST satisfy BOTH:
14	       * score(relevant) > score(junk)            (semantic direction), AND
15	       * score(relevant) != score(junk)           (NOT a constant — a load-fail / OOM / wrong-
16	                                                    template reranker that returns a constant
17	                                                    would pass a bare ">" if both equal; it must
18	                                                    FAIL LOUD, never score a believable number).
19	     A stub / empty / load-fail / missing-key candidate raises RerankerLivenessError (non-zero
20	     exit), it is NEVER scored as "this backend is just bad".
21	
22	  C. LINEAGE (gate0_lineage.py idx binding). The four gold slugs bind to their canonical idx; an
23	     unregistered benchmark slug fails loud.
24	
25	The liveness probe is intentionally LEXICALLY separable (the relevant doc shares query tokens, the
26	junk doc shares none) so the legitimate CPU lexical baseline (token-overlap) ALSO passes — only a
27	stub/constant scorer fails. ``run_liveness_canary`` is a pure function ``(rerank_fn, ...) -> None``
28	so the offline smoke can feed it a constant-stub and prove the gate FAILS correctly.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import math
34	import os
35	import sys
36	from typing import Callable, Sequence
37	
38	_THIS_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
39	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(_THIS_DIR, "..", "..", "..", ".."))
40	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:
41	    sys.path.insert(0, _REPO_ROOT)
42	
43	from scripts.dr_benchmark.retrieval_bakeoff.reranker._lineage_seam import (  # noqa: E402
44	    SLUG_TO_IDX,
45	    GateZeroLineageError,
46	    assert_drb_slug_registered,
47	)
48	
49	# A RerankFn maps (query, list[doc_text]) -> list[float] score, one per doc, HIGHER = more relevant.
50	RerankFn = Callable[[str, Sequence[str]], list[float]]
51	
52	# Floating tolerance for the hand-computed NDCG equality (exact math, generous slack for fp).
53	_NDCG_TOL = 1e-9
54	
55	# The four gold-bearing slugs this layer binds (brief §6). Bound to canonical idx via the seam.
56	GOLD_SLUGS: tuple[str, ...] = (
57	    "drb_72_ai_labor",
58	    "drb_75_metal_ions_cvd",
59	    "drb_76_gut_microbiota_crc",
60	    "drb_78_parkinsons_dbs",
61	)
62	
63	
64	class GateZeroScorerError(RuntimeError):
65	    """Scorer-math canary failed (NDCG / recall@K math is wrong) — every score is invalid."""
66	
67	
68	class RerankerLivenessError(RuntimeError):
69	    """A reranker candidate is non-functional (stub / constant / load-fail) — fail loud, never score."""
70	
71	
72	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
73	# The credibility-graded NDCG@K scorer + the required-source recall@K guard (the scored metric).
74	# These are the EXACT functions run_bakeoff scores with, so the canary tests the real math.
75	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
76	def _dcg(gains: Sequence[float], k: int) -> float:
77	    """Discounted cumulative gain over the first k positions. Standard DCG = sum g_i / log2(i+2)
78	    (i 0-indexed -> rank 1..k uses log2(2)..log2(k+1))."""
79	    total = 0.0
80	    for i, g in enumerate(gains[:k]):
81	        total += float(g) / math.log2(i + 2)
82	    return total
83	
84	
85	def graded_ndcg_at_k(ranked_gains: Sequence[float], k: int) -> float:
86	    """Credibility-GRADED NDCG@K: ``ranked_gains`` are the per-position gains in the ORDER the
87	    reranker produced (gain from the pre-registered table; off-topic == 0). Normalized by the DCG
88	    of the ideal ordering (gains sorted descending). Returns 0.0 when the ideal DCG is 0 (no signal),
89	    never raises on empty input."""
90	    if k <= 0:
91	        raise GateZeroScorerError(f"NDCG@K requires k>0, got k={k}")
92	    ideal = _dcg(sorted((float(g) for g in ranked_gains), reverse=True), k)
93	    if ideal <= 0.0:
94	        return 0.0
95	    return _dcg(ranked_gains, k) / ideal
96	
97	
98	def required_recall_at_k(ranked_required_flags: Sequence[bool], total_required: int, k: int) -> float:
99	    """Fraction of REQUIRED (sole-supporter) sources that survive in the top-K. This is the
100	    non-regression guard: a reranker that evicts a required source below K starves the basket. A
101	    pure re-ORDER count-cut is fine; a SEMANTIC drop is forbidden upstream (the harness never drops).
102	    total_required==0 -> recall is 1.0 (vacuously satisfied)."""
103	    if total_required <= 0:
104	        return 1.0
105	    if k <= 0:
106	        raise GateZeroScorerError(f"recall@K requires k>0, got k={k}")
107	    survived = sum(1 for flag in ranked_required_flags[:k] if flag)
108	    return survived / float(total_required)
109	
110	
111	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
112	# Canary A — scorer math.
113	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
114	def run_scorer_math_canary() -> None:
115	    """Fail loud unless the NDCG + recall math matches HAND-COMPUTED known values.
116	
117	    Ideal case: gains [3,2,1] already sorted -> NDCG@3 == 1.0.
118	    Fractional case (the real discriminator): a SWAP of the top two of [3,2,1,0] to [2,3,1,0].
119	      DCG@4(actual) = 2/log2(2) + 3/log2(3) + 1/log2(4) + 0
120	                    = 2/1 + 3/1.5849625007 + 1/2 + 0 = 2 + 1.8927892607 + 0.5 = 4.3927892607
121	      IDCG@4        = 3/1 + 2/1.5849625007 + 1/2 + 0 = 3 + 1.2618595071 + 0.5 = 4.7618595071
122	      NDCG@4        = 4.3927892607 / 4.7618595071 = 0.9224945117  (hand-computed below)
123	    """
124	    # ---- ideal ordering -> 1.0 (normalization check) ----
125	    ideal = graded_ndcg_at_k([3, 2, 1], k=3)
126	    if abs(ideal - 1.0) > _NDCG_TOL:
127	        raise GateZeroScorerError(f"NDCG ideal-ordering canary: expected 1.0, got {ideal!r}")
128	
129	    # ---- a scorer that always returns 1.0 must NOT pass; the fractional case discriminates ----
130	    log2_3 = math.log2(3)
131	    dcg_actual = 2.0 / 1.0 + 3.0 / log2_3 + 1.0 / 2.0 + 0.0
132	    idcg = 3.0 / 1.0 + 2.0 / log2_3 + 1.0 / 2.0 + 0.0
133	    expected_frac = dcg_actual / idcg
134	    got_frac = graded_ndcg_at_k([2, 3, 1, 0], k=4)
135	    if abs(got_frac - expected_frac) > _NDCG_TOL:
136	        raise GateZeroScorerError(
137	            f"NDCG fractional canary: expected {expected_frac!r}, got {got_frac!r} "
138	            f"(a constant-1.0 scorer is caught here)"
139	        )
140	
141	    # ---- worst ordering (reverse) is strictly < the ideal (and < the fractional swap) ----
142	    worst = graded_ndcg_at_k([0, 1, 2, 3], k=4)
143	    if not (worst < got_frac < 1.0 + _NDCG_TOL):
144	        raise GateZeroScorerError(
145	            f"NDCG monotonicity canary: expected worst({worst!r}) < swap({got_frac!r}) < 1.0"
146	        )
147	
148	    # ---- zero-signal pool -> 0.0, never a divide-by-zero ----
149	    zero_signal = graded_ndcg_at_k([0, 0, 0], k=3)
150	    if abs(zero_signal - 0.0) > _NDCG_TOL:
151	        raise GateZeroScorerError(f"NDCG zero-signal canary: expected 0.0, got {zero_signal!r}")
152	
153	    # ---- required-source recall@K guard math ----
154	    # 4 required sources; ranking keeps 3 of them in top-3, evicts 1 below -> recall == 3/4.
155	    r_kept = required_recall_at_k([True, True, False, True, False], total_required=4, k=3)
156	    if abs(r_kept - (2.0 / 4.0)) > _NDCG_TOL:
157	        # top-3 flags == [True, True, False] -> 2 survived of 4 required.
158	        raise GateZeroScorerError(f"recall@K canary: expected {2/4!r}, got {r_kept!r}")
159	    r_all = required_recall_at_k([True, True, True, True], total_required=4, k=4)
160	    if abs(r_all - 1.0) > _NDCG_TOL:
161	        raise GateZeroScorerError(f"recall@K all-kept canary: expected 1.0, got {r_all!r}")
162	    r_none_required = required_recall_at_k([False, False], total_required=0, k=2)
163	    if abs(r_none_required - 1.0) > _NDCG_TOL:
164	        raise GateZeroScorerError(f"recall@K vacuous canary: expected 1.0, got {r_none_required!r}")
165	
166	
167	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
168	# Canary B — per-candidate model-wiring liveness (the highest-priority anti-drb_72 check).
169	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
170	# A LEXICALLY-separable probe: the relevant doc shares query tokens; the junk doc shares NONE. This
171	# makes the legitimate CPU lexical baseline pass while a stub/constant scorer fails.
172	LIVENESS_QUERY = "deep brain stimulation for parkinson disease motor symptoms"
173	LIVENESS_RELEVANT_DOC = (
174	    "Deep brain stimulation of the subthalamic nucleus improved motor symptoms in patients with "
175	    "advanced parkinson disease, reducing bradykinesia and tremor in a randomized trial."
176	)
177	LIVENESS_JUNK_DOC = (
178	    "The quarterly municipal recycling schedule lists curbside pickup dates for compost bins and "
179	    "reminds residents to flatten cardboard boxes before collection."
180	)
181	
182	
183	def run_liveness_canary(rerank_fn: RerankFn, *, candidate_name: str) -> None:
184	    """Fail loud unless ``rerank_fn`` ranks the obviously-relevant doc STRICTLY above the obvious
185	    junk doc AND the two scores DIFFER. This is a PURE function of the passed callable, so the
186	    offline smoke can feed it a constant-stub and assert it raises.
187	
188	    Raises RerankerLivenessError (non-zero exit at the caller) on ANY of: wrong length, NaN/inf,
189	    constant scores, or relevant<=junk. Never returns a believable low number for a broken model.
190	    """
191	    docs = [LIVENESS_RELEVANT_DOC, LIVENESS_JUNK_DOC]
192	    try:
193	        scores = rerank_fn(LIVENESS_QUERY, docs)
194	    except Exception as exc:  # a load-fail / OOM / missing-key surfaces here -> fail loud
195	        raise RerankerLivenessError(
196	            f"candidate {candidate_name!r} FAILED to score the liveness probe (load/runtime error): "
197	            f"{exc!r}. Excluded as non-functional; never scored."
198	        ) from exc
199	
200	    if not isinstance(scores, (list, tuple)) or len(scores) != 2:
201	        raise RerankerLivenessError(
202	            f"candidate {candidate_name!r} returned {scores!r} (expected 2 numeric scores) — "
203	            f"stub/empty output, excluded as non-functional."
204	        )
205	    try:
206	        s_rel = float(scores[0])
207	        s_junk = float(scores[1])
208	    except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
209	        raise RerankerLivenessError(
210	            f"candidate {candidate_name!r} returned non-numeric scores {scores!r}: {exc!r}"
211	        ) from exc
212	
213	    if not (math.isfinite(s_rel) and math.isfinite(s_junk)):
214	        raise RerankerLivenessError(
215	            f"candidate {candidate_name!r} returned non-finite scores rel={s_rel!r} junk={s_junk!r}"
216	        )
217	    if s_rel == s_junk:
218	        raise RerankerLivenessError(
219	            f"candidate {candidate_name!r} returned a CONSTANT score ({s_rel!r}) for relevant AND "
220	            f"junk — a load-fail / wrong-template / OOM-fallback model. FAIL LOUD (drb_72 class), "
221	            f"never accept a believable-looking NDCG from a constant scorer."
222	        )
223	    if not (s_rel > s_junk):
224	        raise RerankerLivenessError(
225	            f"candidate {candidate_name!r} ranked the JUNK doc >= the relevant doc "
226	            f"(rel={s_rel!r} <= junk={s_junk!r}) — non-functional / rank-inverted, excluded."
227	        )
228	
229	
230	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
231	# Canary C — lineage.
232	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
233	def run_lineage_canary(slugs: Sequence[str] = GOLD_SLUGS) -> None:
234	    """Fail loud unless every gold slug is a registered benchmark slug bound to a canonical idx."""
235	    for slug in slugs:
236	        assert_drb_slug_registered(slug)  # fail loud on an unregistered drb_* slug
237	        if slug not in SLUG_TO_IDX:
238	            raise GateZeroLineageError(
239	                f"GATE0 reranker: gold slug {slug!r} not bound to a canonical idx in SLUG_TO_IDX."
240	            )
241	
242	
243	def run_scorer_and_lineage_gate(slugs: Sequence[str] = GOLD_SLUGS) -> None:
244	    """The offline-safe portion of GATE-0 (no model loads): scorer math + lineage. The per-candidate
245	    liveness canary is run separately by run_bakeoff against each LOADED model."""
246	    run_lineage_canary(slugs)
247	    run_scorer_math_canary()
248	
249	
250	def main() -> int:
251	    try:
252	        run_scorer_and_lineage_gate()
253	    except (GateZeroScorerError, GateZeroLineageError) as exc:
254	        print(f"GATE-0 (scorer+lineage) FAILED: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
255	        return 2
256	    print("GATE-0 scorer-math + lineage canaries GREEN (per-candidate liveness runs in run_bakeoff).")
257	    return 0
258	
259	
260	if __name__ == "__main__":
261	    raise SystemExit(main())
262
