1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-ret-002 (#1294) reranker layer — ISOLATION bake-off runner.
3	
4	Loads each reranker candidate by its EXACT, web-verified HF/pip id, runs the GATE-0 per-candidate
5	LIVENESS canary, then scores it on the FROZEN per-idx pool with credibility-graded NDCG@K +
6	required-source recall@K guard. Writes ranked results JSON.
7	
8	WINNER METRIC (brief §6): credibility-graded NDCG@K is PRIMARY (on POLARIS data); the
9	required-source recall@K guard is a hard NON-REGRESSION gate (a candidate that evicts a required
10	source below the baseline recall@K is disqualified regardless of NDCG — the dedup-0.97-floor
11	pattern). External benchmarks (MTEB-reranking / BEIR) are a SUPPLEMENTAL cross-check only, not run
12	here.
13	
14	RE-ORDER ONLY (§-1.3 / PROD INVARIANT). Every candidate sees the byte-identical frozen pool and the
15	SAME top-K window; they differ ONLY in ORDER. No candidate may drop a row. Off-topic items get gain
16	0 (demoted, they sink in NDCG) — never removed. The off-topic-drop facet is SUPERSEDED/SCRUBBED.
17	
18	HONEST FLAGS:
19	  * needs_gpu candidates are gated behind a live ``torch.cuda.is_available()`` check; with no GPU
20	    they are registered-but-skipped (status="skipped_needs_gpu"), NEVER faked.
21	  * no_key / non-deployable-license candidates are registered honestly (yardstick only; never
22	    crowned). zerank-1-small is Apache-2.0 (deployable); llama-nemotron-rerank-1b-v2 is the NVIDIA
23	    Open Model License YARDSTICK.
24	  * any candidate that fails the liveness canary is recorded status="failed_liveness" and excluded
25	    from the ranking — never scored as a believable low number.
26	
27	The Qwen3-Reranker scoring REUSES the proven causal-LM yes/no-logit method from
28	``scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py`` (loading a Qwen reranker via CrossEncoder mints a random
29	score head -> ~0.5 noise; the canonical method scores P("yes")).
30	"""
31	
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	import argparse
35	import json
36	import os
37	import sys
38	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
39	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional, Sequence
40	
41	_THIS_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
42	_REPO_ROOT = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(_THIS_DIR, "..", "..", "..", ".."))
43	if _REPO_ROOT not in sys.path:
44	    sys.path.insert(0, _REPO_ROOT)
45	
46	from scripts.dr_benchmark.retrieval_bakeoff.reranker import gate0  # noqa: E402
47	from scripts.dr_benchmark.retrieval_bakeoff.reranker import build_fixture  # noqa: E402
48	
49	RerankFn = gate0.RerankFn
50	
51	# Default top-K window (held identical across all candidates). CLI-overridable; never hardcoded as
52	# a "magic" cut — it is the same window every candidate re-orders within.
53	DEFAULT_K = 20
54	
55	
56	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	# Candidate registry — EXACT ids + role + honest runnable flags (brief §6, web-verified).
58	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
59	@dataclass
60	class Candidate:
61	    name: str
62	    model_id: str  # exact HF/pip/in-repo id
63	    role: str  # baseline | candidate | yardstick
64	    license: str
65	    runnable: str  # "yes" (CPU) | "needs_gpu"
66	    deployable: bool  # False => yardstick only, never crowned (license/sovereignty)
67	    loader: str  # which adapter builds the RerankFn
68	    notes: str = ""
69	
70	
71	CANDIDATES: list[Candidate] = [
72	    Candidate(
73	        name="polaris_lexical_rerank",
74	        model_id="src/polaris_graph/retrieval/live_retriever.py:_rerank_and_reserve",
75	        role="baseline",
76	        license="in-repo",
77	        runnable="yes",
78	        deployable=True,
79	        loader="lexical",
80	        notes="current-default FLOOR; token-overlap, CPU, no model. Every model must beat this.",
81	    ),
82	    Candidate(
83	        name="bge_reranker_v2_m3",
84	        model_id="BAAI/bge-reranker-v2-m3",
85	        role="baseline",
86	        license="apache-2.0",
87	        runnable="needs_gpu",
88	        deployable=True,
89	        loader="cross_encoder",
90	        notes="dated 2024 baseline (the old-SOTA line the 2025/26 cohort must clear).",
91	    ),
92	    Candidate(
93	        name="gte_reranker_modernbert_base",
94	        model_id="Alibaba-NLP/gte-reranker-modernbert-base",
95	        role="candidate",
96	        license="apache-2.0",
97	        runnable="needs_gpu",
98	        deployable=True,
99	        loader="cross_encoder",
100	        notes="landscape lead pick (149M, 8192-ctx). CrossEncoder, softmaxed [0,1] scores.",
101	    ),
102	    Candidate(
103	        name="mxbai_rerank_base_v2",
104	        model_id="mixedbread-ai/mxbai-rerank-base-v2",
105	        role="candidate",
106	        license="apache-2.0",
107	        runnable="needs_gpu",
108	        deployable=True,
109	        loader="mxbai",
110	        notes="0.5B RL-tuned instruction-aware; pip install mxbai-rerank; MxbaiRerankV2.rank().",
111	    ),
112	    Candidate(
113	        name="zerank_1_small",
114	        model_id="zeroentropy/zerank-1-small",
115	        role="candidate",
116	        license="apache-2.0",
117	        runnable="needs_gpu",
118	        deployable=True,
119	        loader="cross_encoder_trust",
120	        notes="Apache-2.0 (verified; only zerank-2 is CC-BY-NC). CrossEncoder trust_remote_code.",
121	    ),
122	    Candidate(
123	        name="qwen3_reranker_4b",
124	        model_id="Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-4B",
125	        role="candidate",
126	        license="apache-2.0",
127	        runnable="needs_gpu",
128	        deployable=True,
129	        loader="qwen_causal",
130	        notes="causal-LM yes/no-logit method (NOT CrossEncoder — that mints a random head).",
131	    ),
132	    Candidate(
133	        name="qwen3_reranker_8b",
134	        model_id="Qwen/Qwen3-Reranker-8B",
135	        role="candidate",
136	        license="apache-2.0",
137	        runnable="needs_gpu",
138	        deployable=True,
139	        loader="qwen_causal",
140	        notes="causal-LM yes/no-logit method.",
141	    ),
142	    Candidate(
143	        name="llama_nemotron_rerank_1b_v2",
144	        model_id="nvidia/llama-nemotron-rerank-1b-v2",
145	        role="yardstick",
146	        license="nvidia-open-model-license",
147	        runnable="needs_gpu",
148	        deployable=False,  # NVIDIA OML — yardstick only, never crowned vs the Apache cohort
149	        loader="nemotron",
150	        notes="1B Llama-3.2 cross-encoder yardstick (NVIDIA Open Model License).",
151	    ),
152	]
153	
154	
155	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
156	# Adapters: each returns a RerankFn (query, docs) -> list[float]. Models load lazily, ONCE.
157	# All torch / transformers / vendor imports are INSIDE the adapters so the module imports + the
158	# offline smoke run with NO ML deps installed and NO network.
159	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
160	def _build_lexical_rerank_fn() -> RerankFn:
161	    """Baseline: the in-repo lexical token-overlap score (no model, CPU). Wraps the SAME helpers
162	    the production ``_rerank_and_reserve`` uses so the bake-off scores the real incumbent signal."""
163	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import (  # local import (heavy module)
164	        _lexical_relevance_score,
165	        _rerank_content_tokens,
166	    )
167	
168	    class _Stub:
169	        """Minimal SearchCandidate-shaped object _lexical_relevance_score can score."""
170	
171	        def __init__(self, text: str) -> None:
172	            self.title = text
173	            self.snippet = ""
174	            self.body = text
175	
176	    def fn(query: str, docs: Sequence[str]) -> list[float]:
177	        q_tokens = _rerank_content_tokens(query)
178	        return [float(_lexical_relevance_score(_Stub(d), q_tokens)) for d in docs]
179	
180	    return fn
181	
182	
183	def _build_cross_encoder_fn(model_id: str, *, trust_remote_code: bool, device: str) -> RerankFn:
184	    from sentence_transformers import CrossEncoder
185	
186	    model = CrossEncoder(model_id, trust_remote_code=trust_remote_code, device=device)
187	
188	    def fn(query: str, docs: Sequence[str]) -> list[float]:
189	        pairs = [[query, d] for d in docs]
190	        scores = model.predict(pairs)
191	        return [float(s) for s in scores]
192	
193	    return fn
194	
195	
196	def _build_mxbai_fn(model_id: str, *, device: str) -> RerankFn:
197	    from mxbai_rerank import MxbaiRerankV2
198	
199	    model = MxbaiRerankV2(model_id, device=device)
200	
201	    def fn(query: str, docs: Sequence[str]) -> list[float]:
202	        # .rank returns results ranked; we need a score PER input position, in input order.
203	        results = model.rank(query, list(docs), return_documents=False, top_k=len(docs))
204	        by_index = {int(r.index): float(r.score) for r in results}
205	        return [by_index[i] for i in range(len(docs))]
206	
207	    return fn
208	
209	
210	def _build_qwen_causal_fn(model_id: str, *, device: str) -> RerankFn:
211	    """REUSE the proven causal-LM yes/no-logit method from scripts/relevance_scorer_bakeoff.py."""
212	    import torch
213	    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
214	
215	    tok = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id, padding_side="left")
216	    model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id, dtype="auto").to(device).eval()
217	    tid_no = tok.convert_tokens_to_ids("no")
218	    tid_yes = tok.convert_tokens_to_ids("yes")
219	    prefix = (
220	        "<|im_start|>system\nJudge whether the Document meets the requirements based on the Query "
221	        'and the Instruct provided. Note that the answer can only be "yes" or "no".<|im_end|>\n'
222	        "<|im_start|>user\n"
223	    )
224	    suffix = "<|im_end|>\n<|im_start|>assistant\n<think>\n\n</think>\n\n"
225	    instr = "Given a web search query, retrieve relevant passages that answer the query"
226	
227	    def score_one(q: str, doc: str) -> float:
228	        text = prefix + f"<Instruct>: {instr}\n<Query>: {q}\n<Document>: {doc}" + suffix
229	        inp = tok(text, return_tensors="pt", truncation=True, max_length=4096).to(device)
230	        with torch.no_grad():
231	            logits = model(**inp).logits[0, -1]
232	        pair = torch.stack([logits[tid_no], logits[tid_yes]]).float()
233	        return float(torch.softmax(pair, dim=0)[1])
234	
235	    def fn(query: str, docs: Sequence[str]) -> list[float]:
236	        return [score_one(query, d) for d in docs]
237	
238	    return fn
239	
240	
241	def _build_nemotron_fn(model_id: str, *, device: str) -> RerankFn:
242	    """NVIDIA Llama-Nemotron rerank-1b-v2 — a transformer cross-encoder. Loaded via Sentence
243	    Transformers CrossEncoder with trust_remote_code (per the model card's ST integration)."""
244	    return _build_cross_encoder_fn(model_id, trust_remote_code=True, device=device)
245	
246	
247	def build_rerank_fn(cand: Candidate, *, device: str) -> RerankFn:
248	    if cand.loader == "lexical":
249	        return _build_lexical_rerank_fn()
250	    if cand.loader == "cross_encoder":
251	        return _build_cross_encoder_fn(cand.model_id, trust_remote_code=False, device=device)
252	    if cand.loader == "cross_encoder_trust":
253	        return _build_cross_encoder_fn(cand.model_id, trust_remote_code=True, device=device)
254	    if cand.loader == "mxbai":
255	        return _build_mxbai_fn(cand.model_id, device=device)
256	    if cand.loader == "qwen_causal":
257	        return _build_qwen_causal_fn(cand.model_id, device=device)
258	    if cand.loader == "nemotron":
259	        return _build_nemotron_fn(cand.model_id, device=device)
260	    raise ValueError(f"unknown loader {cand.loader!r} for candidate {cand.name!r}")
261	
262	
263	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
264	# Scoring one candidate over one frozen idx pool (RE-ORDER only).
265	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
266	def _ranked_order(scores: Sequence[float]) -> list[int]:
267	    """Stable descending sort of indices by score (ties keep arrival order — a re-order, not a drop)."""
268	    return sorted(range(len(scores)), key=lambda i: (-float(scores[i]), i))
269	
270	
271	def score_candidate_on_idx(
272	    rerank_fn: RerankFn,
273	    fx: "build_fixture.GoldIdxFixture",
274	    *,
275	    k: int,
276	) -> dict[str, Any]:
277	    """Return {ndcg_at_k, required_recall_at_k, gold_n, required_n, pool_n}. Re-orders the SAME
278	    pool; never removes a row."""
279	    docs = [c.body or c.title for c in fx.pool]
280	    gains = fx.gains()
281	    required_flags = [c.required for c in fx.pool]
282	    total_required = sum(1 for f in required_flags if f)
283	
284	    scores = rerank_fn(fx.question, docs)
285	    if len(scores) != len(docs):
286	        raise gate0.RerankerLivenessError(
287	            f"reranker returned {len(scores)} scores for {len(docs)} docs (re-order must be 1:1)."
288	        )
289	    order = _ranked_order(scores)
290	    ranked_gains = [gains[i] for i in order]
291	    ranked_required = [required_flags[i] for i in order]
292	    return {
293	        "ndcg_at_k": gate0.graded_ndcg_at_k(ranked_gains, k),
294	        "required_recall_at_k": gate0.required_recall_at_k(ranked_required, total_required, k),
295	        "gold_n": len(fx.gold_claim_ids),
296	        "required_n": total_required,
297	        "pool_n": len(fx.pool),
298	    }
299	
300	
301	def run_bakeoff(
302	    *,
303	    fixtures: dict[str, "build_fixture.GoldIdxFixture"],
304	    candidates: list[Candidate],
305	    k: int,
306	    device: str,
307	    have_gpu: bool,
308	) -> dict[str, Any]:
309	    """Run GATE-0 (scorer+lineage) once, then per-candidate liveness + scoring. Returns the full
310	    results dict (also a ranked list). Honest skip/fail statuses; never fabricates a score."""
311	    gate0.run_scorer_and_lineage_gate(tuple(fixtures.keys()))  # fail loud before any model load
312	
313	    results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
314	    baseline_recall: dict[str, float] = {}  # slug -> baseline required-recall@K (non-regression ref)
315	
316	    for cand in candidates:
317	        row: dict[str, Any] = {
318	            "name": cand.name,
319	            "model_id": cand.model_id,
320	            "role": cand.role,
321	            "license": cand.license,
322	            "deployable": cand.deployable,
323	            "status": "pending",
324	        }
325	        # Honest GPU gate.
326	        if cand.runnable == "needs_gpu" and not have_gpu:
327	            row["status"] = "skipped_needs_gpu"
328	            results.append(row)
329	            print(f"[skip] {cand.name}: needs_gpu, no CUDA available (registered, not faked)", flush=True)
330	            continue
331	        # Load + per-candidate liveness canary (fail loud, never score a stub).
332	        try:
333	            fn = build_rerank_fn(cand, device=device)
334	        except Exception as exc:  # missing dep / missing weights / load error
335	            row["status"] = "failed_load"
336	            row["error"] = repr(exc)[:300]
337	            results.append(row)
338	            print(f"[fail-load] {cand.name}: {exc!r}", flush=True)
339	            continue
340	        try:
341	            gate0.run_liveness_canary(fn, candidate_name=cand.name)
342	        except gate0.RerankerLivenessError as exc:
343	            row["status"] = "failed_liveness"
344	            row["error"] = str(exc)[:300]
345	            results.append(row)
346	            print(f"[fail-liveness] {cand.name}: {exc}", flush=True)
347	            continue
348	
349	        # Score every gold idx.
350	        per_idx: dict[str, Any] = {}
351	        ndcgs: list[float] = []
352	        for slug, fx in fixtures.items():
353	            m = score_candidate_on_idx(fn, fx, k=k)
354	            per_idx[slug] = m
355	            ndcgs.append(m["ndcg_at_k"])
356	            if cand.role == "baseline" and cand.name == "polaris_lexical_rerank":
357	                baseline_recall[slug] = m["required_recall_at_k"]
358	        row["status"] = "scored"
359	        row["per_idx"] = per_idx
360	        row["mean_ndcg_at_k"] = sum(ndcgs) / len(ndcgs) if ndcgs else 0.0
361	        results.append(row)
362	        print(f"[scored] {cand.name}: mean_ndcg@{k}={row['mean_ndcg_at_k']:.4f}", flush=True)
363	
364	    # Non-regression guard: disqualify any scored candidate whose required-recall@K regressed below
365	    # the baseline on ANY idx (a re-order that evicts a required sole-supporter). NOT a drop — the
366	    # candidate kept all rows; it just ordered a required source out of the window.
367	    #
368	    # CRITICAL anti-silent-no-op (drb_72 class): the guard is RELATIVE to the lexical baseline. If
369	    # the baseline did not actually run+score (subset run, or it failed_load/failed_liveness), there
370	    # is NO reference recall to compare against — the guard CANNOT be evaluated. We must NOT default
371	    # such candidates to passes_recall_guard=True (a believable-false pass that could crown an
372	    # unvetted reranker). They are marked "not_evaluated" and EXCLUDED from crowning, fail-loud-visible.
373	    scored_rows = [r for r in results if r.get("status") == "scored"]
374	    baseline_ran = bool(baseline_recall) and any(
375	        r["name"] == "polaris_lexical_rerank" and r["status"] == "scored" for r in results
376	    )
377	    if scored_rows and not baseline_ran:
378	        print(
379	            "[recall-guard] WARNING: the polaris_lexical_rerank baseline did NOT score (subset run "
380	            "or load/liveness failure) — the required-recall@K non-regression guard CANNOT be "
381	            "evaluated. Scored candidates are marked passes_recall_guard='not_evaluated' and are "
382	            "EXCLUDED from crowning (never a silent True). Re-run WITH the baseline to crown a winner.",
383	            flush=True,
384	        )
385	    for row in scored_rows:
386	        if not baseline_ran:
387	            row["required_recall_regressions"] = []
388	            row["passes_recall_guard"] = "not_evaluated"
389	            continue
390	        violations = []
391	        for slug, m in row.get("per_idx", {}).items():
392	            base = baseline_recall.get(slug)
393	            if base is None:
394	                # Baseline ran but lacks THIS idx (slug mismatch) — also cannot compare; fail loud.
395	                raise gate0.RerankerLivenessError(
396	                    f"recall-guard: baseline has no recall for idx {slug!r} but candidate "
397	                    f"{row['name']!r} scored it — cannot evaluate the non-regression guard. "
398	                    f"Run baseline+candidates over the SAME slug set."
399	                )
400	            if m["required_recall_at_k"] < base - 1e-12:
401	                violations.append(
402	                    {"slug": slug, "recall": m["required_recall_at_k"], "baseline": base}
403	                )
404	        row["required_recall_regressions"] = violations
405	        row["passes_recall_guard"] = len(violations) == 0
406	        if violations:
407	            print(
408	                f"[recall-guard] {row['name']} DISQUALIFIED: required-recall@{k} regressed vs "
409	                f"baseline on {[v['slug'] for v in violations]}",
410	                flush=True,
411	            )
412	
413	    # Rank: deployable, scored, AND the recall guard returned a real True (not 'not_evaluated').
414	    # Yardsticks/non-deployable are ranked separately (never crowned). Skipped/failed never enter.
415	    eligible = [
416	        r for r in results
417	        if r.get("status") == "scored" and r.get("deployable") and r.get("passes_recall_guard") is True
418	    ]
419	    eligible.sort(key=lambda r: -r["mean_ndcg_at_k"])
420	    for i, r in enumerate(eligible):
421	        r["rank"] = i + 1
422	
423	    return {
424	        "layer": "reranker",
425	        "k": k,
426	        "metric": "credibility_graded_ndcg_at_k (PRIMARY) + required_source_recall_at_k guard",
427	        "off_topic_drop_facet": "SUPERSEDED_SCRUBBED (demote-only, re-order-only, no hard drop)",
428	        "have_gpu": have_gpu,
429	        "device": device,
430	        "candidates": results,
431	        "ranking_deployable_recall_guard_passed": [r["name"] for r in eligible],
432	    }
433	
434	
435	def main() -> int:
436	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run the reranker ISOLATION bake-off.")
437	    ap.add_argument("--pool-dir", required=True)
438	    ap.add_argument("--annot-dir", required=True)
439	    ap.add_argument("--info-recall-dir", required=True)
440	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True, help="results JSON path")
441	    ap.add_argument("--k", type=int, default=DEFAULT_K)
442	    ap.add_argument("--slugs", default=",".join(build_fixture.GOLD_SLUGS))
443	    ap.add_argument(
444	        "--candidates", default=",".join(c.name for c in CANDIDATES),
445	        help="comma-separated candidate names to run (default: all)",
446	    )
447	    args = ap.parse_args()
448	
449	    device = "cpu"
450	    have_gpu = False
451	    try:
452	        import torch
453	        have_gpu = bool(torch.cuda.is_available())
454	        device = "cuda" if have_gpu else "cpu"
455	    except Exception:
456	        pass
457	
458	    slugs = tuple(s for s in args.slugs.split(",") if s)
459	    fixtures = build_fixture.build_all(
460	        slugs=slugs,
461	        pool_dir=args.pool_dir,
462	        annot_dir=args.annot_dir,
463	        info_recall_dir=args.info_recall_dir,
464	    )
465	    wanted = {c.strip() for c in args.candidates.split(",") if c.strip()}
466	    cands = [c for c in CANDIDATES if c.name in wanted]
467	    if not cands:
468	        print(f"no candidates selected from {sorted(wanted)}", file=sys.stderr)
469	        return 2
470	
471	    out = run_bakeoff(fixtures=fixtures, candidates=cands, k=args.k, device=device, have_gpu=have_gpu)
472	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(args.out)), exist_ok=True)
473	    with open(args.out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
474	        json.dump(out, fh, indent=2, default=str)
475	    print(f"\nwrote {args.out}; ranking={out['ranking_deployable_recall_guard_passed']}")
476	    return 0
477	
478	
479	if __name__ == "__main__":
480	    raise SystemExit(main())

4	Proves, with synthetic data and MOCKED model/API loaders (NO GPU, NO network, NO model download):

82	def test_liveness_canary_fails_on_stub() -> None:

84	    lexical-style stub PASSES."""
